scenarios.scenario-25: TabStop(position_inches=0) and TabStop(position_inches=-1.5) raise ValueError mentioning '> 0'
scenarios.scenario-26: Paragraph with default empty tab_stops list does not allocate any python-docx tab_stops collection entries
scenarios.scenario-27: First Run with footnote='text' bootstraps a /word/footnotes.xml package part with the canonical separator (id=-1) + continuationSeparator (id=0) entries; the user footnote lives at id=1 with the supplied body text
scenarios.scenario-28: N consecutive footnoted runs share one footnotes part; user footnote ids increment 1, 2, ..., N; matching number of w:footnoteReference elements appear in the document body, each pointing to a distinct id
scenarios.scenario-29: Run(text, footnote='') and Run(text, footnote=' ') raise ValueError mentioning 'non-empty'

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Content][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT] — fires per Paragraph or Image .render() call

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Paragraph or Image must NEVER write to the filesystem during render — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Run must not be re-used across Paragraphs (would corrupt OOXML). Render() may consume the Run; subsequent renders raise.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Per-run overrides set to None must NOT clobber the inherited Style. Only an explicit non-None override value reaches python-docx font properties.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Bookmark id allocator must scan the entire body for max existing id before emitting; never restart from 0 mid-document.

V-MP-CONTENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1','2']]).render(doc) creates 2 rows x 2 cols; first row treated as header when header=True (default)
scenarios.scenario-2: Table.from_markdown('| a | b |\n|---|---|\n| 1 | 2 |') produces equivalent table
scenarios.scenario-3: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1,'b':2},{'a':3,'b':4}]) emits 3 rows x 2 cols (header + 2 data rows); column order follows insertion order of first dict
scenarios.scenario-4: Table.financial(rows) right-aligns numeric cells AND applies thousands separator (1000 -> '1,000')
scenarios.scenario-5: Table.comparison(left='Q1', right='Q2', rows=[...]) produces a 2-column table with left/right headers
scenarios.scenario-6: apply_style(preset_table) overrides cell style; set_column_widths([2,3]) sets column widths in inches; autofit=True sets the python-docx table autofit flag
scenarios.scenario-7: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1']]) raises TABLE_RAGGED_ROWS naming the bad row index
scenarios.scenario-8: Table.from_markdown('not a markdown table') raises TABLE_MARKDOWN_PARSE_ERROR
scenarios.scenario-9: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1},{'b':2}]) raises TABLE_INVALID_DICT_KEYS naming the diverging keys (rows must share the same key set)
scenarios.scenario-10: Cell(value, colspan=N) on a row with N+ columns merges the primary cell with the next N-1 cells; merged python-docx cells share a single _tc element; top-left content survives (covered cells' content is silently dropped per python-docx merge semantics)
scenarios.scenario-11: Cell(value, rowspan=M) on row r merges (r,c) with (r+M-1,c); python-docx _tc identity test confirms the merge
scenarios.scenario-12: Cell(value, rowspan=M, colspan=N) merges the M x N rectangle; all M*N python-docx cells share the same _tc; cells outside the region remain independent
scenarios.scenario-13: Cell(value, colspan=0) or Cell(value, rowspan=-1) raises TABLE_MERGE_INVALID_SPAN at construction
scenarios.scenario-14: Cell(rowspan=2) on the last row, or Cell(colspan=N+1) at column c with c+N >= col_count, raises TABLE_MERGE_OUT_OF_BOUNDS at render time naming the offending grid coordinates

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Table][render][BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE] — fires once per Table.render() call; payload: row_count, col_count

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Factories must validate row shape before constructing Table — never return a partially-built Table that would raise mid-render
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: financial() must not silently coerce non-numeric cells; non-numerics keep their raw value and skip thousands separator
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Render-time merge validation must NEVER produce malformed OOXML — out-of-bounds spans must raise BEFORE writing the merge, leaving the in-progress python-docx Table in a state the caller can discard

V-MP-TABLE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction + render onto per-test Document; no shared state

scenarios.scenario-1: Section('Title', level=1).add_paragraph('text') returns self (fluent chain works)
scenarios.scenario-2: Section.render(doc) emits a w:p with heading style matching level (Heading 1, Heading 2, ...) followed by the content blocks in order
scenarios.scenario-3: add_paragraph accepts both str (shorthand) and Paragraph instance
scenarios.scenario-4: Section('X', level=10) raises SECTION_LEVEL_OUT_OF_RANGE (valid: 1..6)
scenarios.scenario-5: (Phase-8 unstub commit caf30ab) Section('X', level=1).add_chart(chart: Chart) appends Chart to _blocks AND returns self for fluent chaining. Pre-Phase-8 raised NotImplementedError + emitted BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD; that stub-era contract is retired. NOTE: Section.add_chart with a non-Chart input may raise TypeError via mypy/runtime checks but the legacy "Phase 2" message is no longer expected.
scenarios.scenario-6: (Phase-8) Section.render walks Chart.render — saving a Section that contains a Chart produces an inline_shape whose width.emu == round(width_inches × 914400). The legacy BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD-on-add_chart trace from Phase-7 is retired; Chart blocks emit their own [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] marker via the per-block render dispatch (no Section-side marker on add_chart anymore).

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] — fires on stub touch; payload: method + target_phase

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: add_chart must raise immediately after emitting BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD — never silently no-op
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Fluent methods must NEVER mutate other Section instances — each .add_* mutates self only

V-MP-SECTION.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction and per-test render

scenarios.scenario-1: Callout(text, kind=INFO).render emits a 1x1 python-docx Table; cell shading fill = "EBF5FB"; cell left border color = "2E75B6"; default title "Info" appears in cell text
scenarios.scenario-2: Callout(text, kind=WARNING).render uses the amber palette: shading "FFF8E1", left border "E8A838"; default title "Warning"
scenarios.scenario-3: Callout(text, kind=CODE).render uses grey palette ("F5F5F5" shading, "DDDDDD" border) and switches the body run's font to Courier New
scenarios.scenario-4: Explicit title kwarg overrides the kind default — the default title is NOT emitted when title is provided
scenarios.scenario-5: render emits exactly one [MP-Callout][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CALLOUT] marker carrying kind and has_title
scenarios.scenario-6: Section.add_callout(callout) appends to _blocks, returns self; Section.render walks Callout.render so the callout's table appears in document order

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Callout][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CALLOUT] — fires once per Callout.render call; payload: kind, has_title

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: render() must NEVER write to the filesystem — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: The python-docx-seeded empty paragraph in the new cell must be removed before our managed paragraphs are added — leaving it produces a leading blank line above the title

V-MP-CALLOUT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: List(['a','b','c']).render(doc) emits 3 paragraphs each with python-docx 'List Bullet' style and the corresponding text
scenarios.scenario-2: List(items, kind=ListKind.NUMBERED).render emits paragraphs with 'List Number' style
scenarios.scenario-3: List(items, kind=ListKind.CHECKLIST).render prefixes each item text with the unicode ☐ + space; underlying paragraph style is 'List Bullet' (no native checkbox content-control in v0.1)
scenarios.scenario-4: List(items=[]).render emits zero paragraphs (empty lists are silently allowed for conditional rendering)
scenarios.scenario-5: List(items, level=N) sets paragraph_format.left_indent to Inches(0.25 * N) for each emitted paragraph
scenarios.scenario-6: List(items, level=-1) raises LIST_LEVEL_OUT_OF_RANGE at construction time
scenarios.scenario-7: render emits exactly one [MP-List][render][BLOCK_RENDER_LIST] marker carrying kind, items count, and level
scenarios.scenario-8: Section.add_list(list_block) appends to _blocks, returns self; Section.render walks List.render so list paragraphs appear in document order

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-List][render][BLOCK_RENDER_LIST] — fires once per List.render call; payload: kind, items count, level

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: render() must NEVER write to the filesystem — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Mutating List.items between renders must NOT corrupt previously rendered paragraphs (python-docx materializes immediately on add_paragraph)

V-MP-LIST.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: Document(format='docx', title='X').with_style_preset('alga_corporate') returns self
scenarios.scenario-2: add_cover/add_section/add_toc/set_header/set_footer all return self for chaining; stored in declared order
scenarios.scenario-3: save(tmp_path / 'out.docx') produces a file that exists; lxml parses word/document.xml without error
scenarios.scenario-4: Document(format='pdf') raises DOCUMENT_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED naming valid formats {'docx'} for Phase-1
scenarios.scenario-5: Document(...).with_style_preset('does_not_exist') raises DOCUMENT_PRESET_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-6: All former stubs are now concrete: validate → ValidationReport, fix → FixReport, inject_grace → GRACEManifest, to_pdf → Path. Zero NotImplementedError raisers. Zero BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD emitters.
scenarios.scenario-7: save() emits exactly one [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] with section_count, table_count, output_path payload
scenarios.scenario-8: IDEMPOTENCY UNDER M-FINGERPRINT: invoking save() twice on the same Document produces two files whose `mint.fingerprint.fingerprint(path).hash` are equal. Pytest snippet (lives in tests/unit/test_mp_document.py): ``` from mint.fingerprint import fingerprint doc = build_doc(...) doc.save(tmp_path/"a.docx"); doc.save(tmp_path/"b.docx") assert fingerprint(tmp_path/"a.docx").hash == fingerprint(tmp_path/"b.docx").hash ``` Byte-equality is NOT asserted — python-docx writes attributes in non-deterministic order; the M-FINGERPRINT structural hash is the canonical equivalence relation. Failure mode: if hashes differ across two save() calls of the same Document, save() carries hidden non-determinism (e.g. uuid in cover, timestamp in header) — the test points at the diverging part by re-running with fingerprint().xml_sources to isolate which part of the ZIP changed.
scenarios.scenario-9: AUDIT-BASELINE PIN (Wave-7-5 e2e shape; mirrored here for module-local sanity): the golden 3-section document built per VF-013 produces a structural fingerprint that matches `tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json::structural_fingerprint_sha256`. First-ever run writes the baseline (with explicit `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` env, gated to prevent accidental overwrites). Subsequent runs assert equality; on mismatch the failure prints the new vs. old hash AND the diff of fingerprint().xml_sources to localize the regression.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] — fires once per save
required-log-markers.marker-2: RETIRED post-Phase-12 — BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD no longer fires; all Document stubs concrete

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: save() must never produce a partial file: on serialization error, the temp output is removed before re-raising
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX firing more than once for a single save() call is forbidden (would indicate retry without state cleanup)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SWARM-SAFETY: MP-DOCUMENT must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton. Document instances are explicit per-call; no `_default_document` or registry. Tests MUST use tmp_path for every save target — never write to a stable repo path. Enforcement: `grep -rn 'output\.docx\|/tmp/[a-z]*\.docx' tests/unit/test_mp_document.py tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py` returns zero matches outside tmp_path fixtures.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: save() output must NEVER include GRACE Custom XML Parts in Phase-7 (those are Phase-5/MP-GRACE territory). VF-013 inv-3 enforces this; a regression here would silently break the Phase-5 contract.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: NON-DETERMINISM: save() must NOT introduce timestamps, UUIDs, or random IDs into the output beyond what python-docx itself emits at fixed values. If python-docx ever starts emitting per-call timestamps (e.g. for core.xml created/modified fields), MP-DOCUMENT MUST override these to a fixed sentinel value to preserve idempotency under M-FINGERPRINT.

field.name: schema_version
field.description: Baseline format version; bumped on shape changes

field.name: committed_at
field.description: ISO date of baseline commit; informational

field.name: generator
field.description: Tool versions used to generate baseline

field.name: document_spec
field.description: Reference to the function that builds the golden doc; future agents must rebuild via this same function

field.name: xml_sources
field.description: Parts contributing to the fingerprint hash; recorded so a future drift can be localized. NOTE: M-FINGERPRINT for python-docx output yields the styles.xml + numbering.xml subset shown here — NOT the docx-js-era list (document.xml + styles.xml + theme1.xml). See tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json for the canonical Phase-7 baseline value.

field.name: counts
field.description: Structural counts from BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX payload + lxml introspection

field.name: audit_violations
field.description: M-VALIDATE audit-mode violations grouped by rule_id+severity (NOT individual instances — instances drift across python-docx versions; aggregated counts are stable)

field.name: hard_violation_count
field.description: MUST be 0 for VF-013 lenient acceptance; tracked separately for fast-fail

field.name: schema_validation
field.description: lxml_parses asserts word/document.xml parses without raising; ecma376_validated=false for Phase-7 (full ECMA-376 schema validation deferred to Phase-3)

baseline-update-protocol.step-1: Set `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` in the test invocation env to allow overwrite (default is read-only assertion).
baseline-update-protocol.step-2: Manually inspect the diff: structural_fingerprint_sha256 change WITHOUT a corresponding intentional MP-* change is a regression flag, not a baseline update.
baseline-update-protocol.step-3: Commit the new baseline in the same PR that ships the corresponding MP-* code change; commit message MUST reference the rationale (e.g. "MP-DOCUMENT: switch heading rendering from Title to Heading 1; baseline rotated").
baseline-update-protocol.step-4: Audit-violation rotation: a baseline that grows hard_violation_count above 0 is a Phase-7 acceptance failure — no rotation; fix the regression.

V-MP-DOCUMENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — save() targets tmp_path per test; no shared mutable state. e2e wave (Wave-7-5) reads-only against M-VALIDATE; baseline assertion is deterministic. Worker-write to tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json is gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env so concurrent workers cannot race-overwrite.

implementation-path.source: src/mint_python/core/chart.py — Chart class definition + 7 factories + from_matplotlib/seaborn/plotly + render. The class lives in CORE per repo convention (Phase-7 established this with Document/Section/Table/Style/Image). MP-SDK re-exports Chart in src/mint_python/sdk/__init__.py (Phase-8 Wave-8-2 step-3); users write `from mint_python.sdk import Chart` but the class identity is `mint_python.core.chart.Chart`.
implementation-path.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (legacy js engine — V-MP-PKG forbidden-2 invariant); src/mint_python/sdk/__init__.py (Wave-8-2 worker scope, NOT Wave-8-1); any other src/mint_python/core/* file (Phase-8 doesn't modify sibling modules).

scenarios.scenario-1: Chart.bar(labels, values, title, width_inches) returns a Chart instance whose chart_type=='bar', _png_bytes is non-empty, and width_inches matches input
scenarios.scenario-2: Chart.line(labels, values, title, width_inches) — same shape verification with chart_type=='line'
scenarios.scenario-3: Chart.stacked_bar(labels, series_dict, ...) accepts dict-of-series shape and produces stacked rendering (verify via matplotlib axes inspection if feasible OR PNG bytes non-empty + chart_type tag)
scenarios.scenario-4: Chart.pie(labels, values, ...) — verify wedge count matches len(values) via figure introspection before render closes
scenarios.scenario-5: Chart.heatmap(matrix, row_labels, col_labels, ...) — matrix shape mismatch with labels raises CHART_INVALID_DATA
scenarios.scenario-6: Chart.waterfall(labels, deltas, ...) — verify sum-of-deltas annotation present (composed bar primitive)
scenarios.scenario-7: Chart.gantt(tasks, ...) — verify horizontal-bar shape; tasks=[(name, start, duration), ...] minimum schema
scenarios.scenario-8: Chart.from_matplotlib(fig, caption, width_inches, vector=True) wraps user Figure; vector kwarg accepted and stored on instance but Phase-8 PNG-only — assert _png_bytes non-empty + caption attached
scenarios.scenario-9: Chart.from_seaborn(fig, **kwargs) — when seaborn installed, aliases to from_matplotlib; when seaborn ImportError, raises ImportError with "pip install seaborn" hint. Test parametrized with monkeypatch on sys.modules to simulate the missing-seaborn case.
scenarios.scenario-10: (Phase-12 unstub commit 7ca3ec3) Chart.from_plotly(plotly_fig) calls fig.to_image(format="png", scale=2) via the kaleido backend; returns Chart with chart_type="plotly" and non-empty _png_bytes. Pre-Phase-12 raised NotImplementedError + emitted BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD; that stub-era contract is retired.
scenarios.scenario-10b: When plotly is missing from sys.modules, Chart.from_plotly raises ImportError naming "plotly kaleido" install hint. When plotly imports OK but fig.to_image fails (kaleido absent), raises ImportError pointing at kaleido. Both error paths surface as ImportError, NOT NotImplementedError — the Phase-9+ stub semantics are gone.
scenarios.scenario-11: Each factory emits [MP-Chart][<factory_name>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] exactly once with chart_type and data_shape payload — assert via marker_counter and explicit caplog inspection
scenarios.scenario-12: Chart.render(parent_doc) emits [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] chart_type=<name> emu_width=<int> exactly once — verify emu_width = round(width_inches × 914400)
scenarios.scenario-13: Chart.render produces python-docx inline_shape with width.emu == round(width_inches × 914400) (asserted on a fresh DocxDocument)
scenarios.scenario-14: CORPORATE THEMING: build a Chart under alga_corporate preset; matplotlib rcParams snapshot before/after the build call shows font.family and color cycle changes (or, if rcParams not exposed, assert that the active palette resolved to alga primary hex via figure axis line color)
scenarios.scenario-15: CHARTJUNK: rendered Chart's matplotlib axes have spines.top.set_visible(False) and spines.right.set_visible(False) — verify by retaining a hook to the figure or by snapshot of figure XML before close
scenarios.scenario-16: CHART_INVALID_DATA: Chart.bar(labels=["a","b"], values=[1.0]) raises with message naming the length mismatch (len(labels)=2, len(values)=1)

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Chart][<factory>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] — fires per factory call (one per: bar, line, stacked_bar, pie, heatmap, waterfall, gantt) AND once per from_matplotlib/from_seaborn call (chart_type captured from method name)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Chart construction must NEVER write to filesystem — PNG bytes are cached in-memory in the Chart instance
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: matplotlib backend MUST be 'Agg' at import time — Chart module forces matplotlib.use('Agg') before any Figure construction (no display dep)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SEABORN MUST NOT be imported at module level — only inside Chart.from_seaborn lazy-import. Verify with `python -c "import mint_python.core.chart; assert 'seaborn' not in sys.modules"`
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Plotly stub MUST raise BEFORE any matplotlib invocation — never returns a partially-built Chart instance
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: NON-REGRESSION: Chart module must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton (matplotlib rcParams modification at module load is forbidden — themes apply per-construct via context manager OR per-call rcParams override that does not leak)

V-MP-CHART.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES: matplotlib backend is process-local (Agg forced); rcParams modifications inside `with mpl.rc_context(...):` blocks don't leak across tests; tmp_path mandate for any image writes; seaborn lazy-import keeps the dep optional. Mid-risk due to matplotlib-version variance — pin matplotlib>=3.7 to stabilize rcParams behavior.

scenarios.scenario-1: All §3 names import: `from mint_python.sdk import Document, Section, Table, Style, Image, TOC, Pt, ColorPalette, presets` exits 0
scenarios.scenario-2: presets registry contains exactly the agreed Phase-1 entries: 'alga_corporate', 'minimal', 'compact'; presets['alga_corporate'] is a path-or-dict resolvable by load_preset
scenarios.scenario-3: with_style_from(tmp_json) on a Document instance loads the preset and returns self for chaining
scenarios.scenario-4: SDK module re-exports do NOT shadow underlying modules — `mint_python.sdk.Document is mint_python.core.document.Document`

required-log-markers.marker-1: (none — SDK is a re-export module; downstream modules log)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SDK must NOT export anything beyond the §3 surface — extras risk divergence from handover spec
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: SDK must NOT import from src/mint/* (legacy) — the skeleton stays decoupled per V-MP-PKG forbidden-2

V-MP-SDK.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure re-export + small registry; no I/O

V-MP-RULES.source: src/mint_python/rules/ — pure Python successor to M-RULES (src/mint/rules/engine.py). Replaces the Phase-6 empty skeleton with concrete Rule/Violation types, YAML loader, XPath evaluate engine, and all_rules registry.
V-MP-RULES.note: Shared YAML rule files live at rules/d-hard.yaml, rules/d-soft.yaml, rules/p-hard.yaml — same path M-RULES reads. MP-RULES does NOT duplicate these files; it reads the same YAML. The ALLOWED cross-package dependency from src/mint_python/rules/ to rules/ is a documented exception to forbidden-2 (V-MP-PKG constraint-8 / V-MP-STYLE forbidden-2), justified by the shared-config contract that avoids YAML duplication across engines.
V-MP-RULES.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (forbidden-2: no legacy js-engine imports); src/mint_python/core/* (MP-RULES is a leaf utility with no MP-* production deps); docs/*.xml (controller-owned); tests/unit/conftest.py; tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SILENT-XPATH-ERROR: evaluate() raises an uncaught lxml XPathError when given an invalid XPath — forbidden. Must catch etree.XPathError (any type: syntax, namespace, type error) and log at WARNING with rule_id + xpath, then return None.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: RULE-DUPLICATION: hardcoded rule definitions inside source (vs. loaded from YAML). The single YAML source of truth at rules/*.yaml is shared between M-RULES and MP-RULES.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: NO-LOG-ON-FAILURE: all_rules() swallows a broken YAML file without raising RuleLoadError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: SCALAR-RESULT-PASSTHROUGH: evaluate() returns a scalar (number, string, bool) from XPath evaluation instead of wrapping it in a list before passing to check logic. Must normalize tree.xpath() result via isinstance check (list→keep, else→[result]).
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: UNPROTECTED-ALL_RULES: all_rules() reads a YAML file without catching yaml.YAMLError — leaks raw parse exception instead of raising RuleLoadError.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Rules][evaluate][BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE] — fires once per evaluate() call; payload: rule_id, check kind. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE / # END_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
required-log-markers.marker-2: [MP-Rules][all_rules][BLOCK_LOAD_RULES] — fires once per all_rules() call; payload: doc_format, rule_count. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES / # END_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.rules import all_rules; r = all_rules(doc_format='docx'); assert len(r) > 0"
module-checks.check-5: python -c "from mint_python.rules import all_rules; r = all_rules(doc_format='pptx'); assert len(r) > 0"
module-checks.check-6: python -c "import mint_python.rules; assert all(n in dir(mint_python.rules) for n in ['Rule','Violation','Severity','FixCategory','evaluate','all_rules','classify_severity','get_hint'])"

scenarios.scenario-1: YAML loading DOCX: all_rules(doc_format='docx') returns list[Rule] from d-hard.yaml + d-soft.yaml; each Rule has id, format='docx', severity, xpath, check, fix_category, hint, description. Load from rules/ directory (same path as M-RULES).
scenarios.scenario-2: YAML loading PPTX: all_rules(doc_format='pptx') returns list[Rule] from p-hard.yaml. D-H* rules excluded.
scenarios.scenario-3: evaluate 'exists': rule D-H03 (xpath=//w:tblW[@w:type='pct'], check=exists) on docx containing percentage-width table returns Violation. On clean docx returns None (false-positive guard).
scenarios.scenario-4: evaluate 'count_gt_zero': rule P-H03 (xpath=//p:sp[...], check=count_gt_zero) on slide XML with shapes → Violation; on slide with zero matching elements → None.
scenarios.scenario-5: evaluate 'tbl_width_mismatch': D-H01 on bad_column_widths.docx → Violation. On minimal_valid.docx → None. Edge cases: table missing tblGrid → None; table with w:type='auto' → None; negative gridCol/@w values → filtered per lstrip('-').isdigit().
scenarios.scenario-6: evaluate 'sum_mismatch': on XML with mismatched sum → Violation; matching sum → None. Non-numeric text values in sum elements → None (ValueError/TypeError caught, no raise).
scenarios.scenario-7: XPath error: evaluate() with invalid xpath (syntax, namespace, type error) logs [MP-Rules][evaluate][BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE] at WARNING with rule_id + xpath, returns None (never raises). Mirrors M-RULES engine.py:103-108.
scenarios.scenario-8: evaluate 'exists' negative on PPTX: P-H02 (xpath=//a:rPr/a:latin[contains(@typeface,'Times')], check=exists) on bad_font.pptx → Violation. On minimal_valid.pptx → None.
scenarios.scenario-9: classify_severity + get_hint: classify_severity(Violation(severity=HARD, fix_category=DESTRUCTIVE)) → (HARD, DESTRUCTIVE); classify_severity(Violation(severity=SOFT, fix_category=VISUAL)) → (SOFT, VISUAL). get_hint('D-H01') returns dict with rule/reason/fix_instruction; get_hint('UNKNOWN-999') returns dict with reason='Unknown rule'.
scenarios.scenario-10: Trace: all_rules() emits BLOCK_LOAD_RULES at INFO with doc_format + rule_count. evaluate() emits BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE at INFO (per-call, for every rule evaluated regardless of violation result — new contract for MP-RULES, distinct from M-RULES WARNING-only on error). XPath errors log at WARNING with BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
scenarios.scenario-11: Broken YAML: all_rules(rules_dir=dir_with_broken_yaml) raises RuleLoadError with the file path.
scenarios.scenario-12: Missing rules key: YAML with no 'rules' top-level key raises RuleLoadError.
scenarios.scenario-13: Empty rules dir: all_rules(rules_dir=empty_dir) returns empty list — no error.
scenarios.scenario-14: evaluate result wrapping: evaluate() must wrap non-list XPath results (e.g. number, string) via isinstance check → [result]. Mirrors M-RULES engine.py:110.

V-MP-RULES.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure deterministic utility; no mutable state; read-only shared rules/*.yaml. No MP-* production deps.

V-MP-VALIDATE.source: src/mint_python/validate.py — pure Python successor to M-VALIDATE (src/mint/validate.py). Validation engine: opens .docx zip, parses word/document.xml via lxml, runs all_rules, classifies violations, produces ValidationReport.
V-MP-VALIDATE.note: MP-VALIDATE works on saved .docx files (zip + lxml path), NOT on in-memory python-docx Document objects. This mirrors M-VALIDATE's approach and guarantees that validation checks the final serialized OOXML output. The temp-file delegation pattern (Document.validate → save temp → MP-VALIDATE.validate) is the integration bridge at Wave-9-4.
V-MP-VALIDATE.depends: MP-RULES (for Rule, Violation, Severity, FixCategory, evaluate, all_rules, get_hint)
V-MP-VALIDATE.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (forbidden-2); mint.config (MP-VALIDATE defines its own SeverityMode enum — decoupled from legacy M-CONFIG); docs/*.xml; tests/unit/conftest.py; tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SILENT-PARSE-FAILURE: _open_document_xml on broken zip or missing word/document.xml returns None — forbidden. Must raise InvalidDocumentError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: WILDCARD-EXCEPTION-CATCH: broad except swallowing parse errors without re-raise.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: NO-LOG-ON-REPORT: run_checks() produces report with no INFO log of mode + counts.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] — fires once per run_checks(); payload: mode, violations, hard_count, soft_count, passed. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / # END_BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.validate import validate; r = validate('tests/fixtures/minimal_valid.docx'); assert r.passed"
module-checks.check-5: python -c "from mint_python.validate import validate; r = validate('tests/fixtures/bad_column_widths.docx'); assert len([v for v in r.violations if v.rule_id == 'D-H01']) >= 1"

scenarios.scenario-1: AUDIT always passes: validate(minimal_valid.docx) → report.passed=True regardless of violations. The report contains violations but AUDIT mode is observational only.
scenarios.scenario-2: LENIENT mode: clean doc → passed=True. bad_column_widths.docx (D-H01 HARD) → passed=False. Soft violations in clean doc → still passed=True (LENIENT tolerates SOFT).
scenarios.scenario-3: STRICT mode: clean doc → passed=True. Any violation (hard or soft) → passed=False.
scenarios.scenario-4: ValidationReport properties: hard_violations, soft_violations filter correctly; total matches len(violations).
scenarios.scenario-5: classify_violations: groups by severity+fix_category. Empty violations list → all groups empty (no KeyError).
scenarios.scenario-6: Invalid document: run_checks() catches InvalidDocumentError from _open_document_xml and returns ValidationReport with XML-001 pseudo-rule violation (passed=False, hard_count=1). The top-level validate() returns this report — does NOT propagate the exception to caller. This matches legacy M-VALIDATE contract. Document not found, bad ZIP, broken XML inside — all three failure modes produce XML-001 report.
scenarios.scenario-7: Trace: run_checks() emits BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS at INFO before returning. Payload: mode, violations count, hard_count, soft_count, passed boolean.
scenarios.scenario-8: Golden doc lenient: validate(golden_doc.docx) → passed=True, hard_count=0.
scenarios.scenario-9: No matching rules for format: validate(pptx_file) with rules_dir containing only D-H* rules → 0 violations, passed=True.
scenarios.scenario-10: PPTX validation with matching rules: validate(minimal_valid.pptx) with rules_dir including p-hard.yaml → passed=True (AUDIT). validate(bad_font.pptx) → report contains P-H02 violation (non-embedded system font). _get_main_xml_path("pptx") returns "ppt/slides/slide1.xml".
scenarios.scenario-11: Format detection: validate(some_file.docx) detects docx format → reads word/document.xml. validate(some_file.pptx) detects pptx format → reads ppt/slides/slide1.xml. Unsupported extension (e.g. .xlsx) → InvalidDocumentError caught → XML-001 report.

V-MP-VALIDATE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES — tmp_path for saved docs; read-only shared rules/*.yaml.

V-MP-FIX.source: src/mint_python/fix.py — pure Python successor to M-FIX (src/mint/fix.py). Auto-fix engine: backup → iterate safe/visual fixes → re-validate → cascade detection. Destructive fixes rejected.
V-MP-FIX.note: Scope limitation (known from M-FIX): _apply_simple_fix() only implements fix logic for D-H09 (raw newline replacement). All other safe/visual rules (D-H01, D-H04..D-H08, D-H10, D-S01..D-S05) have no concrete fix implementation in Phase-9 scope. _apply_simple_fix() returns False for non-D-H09 rule_ids — this is the documented contract. Extending fix coverage to other rules is a future phase task.
V-MP-FIX.depends: MP-VALIDATE (run_checks, ValidationReport, InvalidDocumentError, SeverityMode); MP-RULES (FixCategory, Violation, Severity)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: MODIFY-WITHOUT-BACKUP: apply_fixes() modifies document without create_backup() first.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: SILENT-DESTRUCTIVE: DESTRUCTIVE violation processed without raising DestructiveRejectedError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: INFINITE-LOOP: iterates beyond max_iterations without CascadeDetectedError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: STALE-FIX-SET: re-uses same violation list across iterations without re-validating.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] — fires once per create_backup(); payload: backup_path. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP / # END_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP.
required-log-markers.marker-2: [MP-Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] — fires once per apply_fixes(); payload: iterations, applied_fixes, diff. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX / # END_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.fix import fix, DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS; assert DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS == 3"

scenarios.scenario-1: Backup created: create_backup(doc) creates .bak with identical content.
scenarios.scenario-2: Safe fix D-H09: raw_newline.docx → FixReport with iterations≥1, applied_fixes containing 'D-H09'. Post-fix re-validation shows 0 D-H09 violations.
scenarios.scenario-3: Destructive rejected: D-H02 (symbol font, destructive) raises DestructiveRejectedError before file modification.
scenarios.scenario-4: Cascade detected: apply_fixes with max_iterations=2 on a violation set where fix A reverses fix B across iterations (file hash oscillates: H-iter-1 ≠ H-iter-2 but H-iter-2 == H-iter-original). CascadeDetectedError raised after iteration_count exceeds max_iterations with no convergence. The detection uses original_hash comparison (M-FIX parity: engine.py:178 — `new_hash == original_hash`). MP-FIX optionally improves this to previous-iteration hash in a follow-up, but the initial ship matches M-FIX behavior.
scenarios.scenario-5: No violations: empty violations → FixReport iterations=1, applied_fixes=[], diff="no changes".
scenarios.scenario-6: Trace: BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP + BLOCK_APPLY_FIX fired with correct payloads.
scenarios.scenario-7: fix() convenience: fix(doc_path) → run_checks → apply_fixes → FixReport.

V-MP-FIX.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES — ALL mutations target tmp_path copies; backup always first; destructive rejected; cascade bounded.

scenarios.scenario-1: Heading mapping: source contains `# H1\n## H2\n### H3` → ArticleSpec has 3 SpecSection entries with level 1, 2, 3 respectively. Title fallback is the first H1 text when title_override is None.
scenarios.scenario-2: GFM table: source `| a | b |\n|:--|--:|\n| 1 | 2 |` parses into one TableBlock with header=("a", "b"), rows=(("1", "2"),). Column alignment from `:--` / `--:` is captured but not yet rendered (alignment plumbing is a follow-up; scenario-2 just asserts the alignment metadata is in attrs).
scenarios.scenario-3:
Fenced code: ```python
x=1
``` block parses into a CodeBlock with language="python" and content="x=1
".
scenarios.scenario-4: Blockquote: `> note text` parses into a CalloutBlock with kind="info" and body="note text". Multi-line quotes preserve their internal newlines as paragraph breaks within the body string.
scenarios.scenario-5: List kinds: bulleted (`- a`), ordered (`1. a`), and task (`- [ ] a`) lists each parse to a ListBlock with the matching ListKind (BULLET / NUMBERED / CHECKLIST). Items keep their plain-text content; inline emphasis within items is preserved as text for v0.
scenarios.scenario-6: Inline emphasis: paragraph with `**bold phrase**` parses into a ParagraphBlock with text containing the phrase verbatim and emphasis=("bold phrase",). Italic-only emphasis is captured similarly. Mixed inline (bold inside italic) records the longest matching substring.
scenarios.scenario-7: Malformed input: source with unbalanced fence (` ```python\nx=1\n` — no closing fence) does NOT raise. markdown-it-py recovers by emitting a fenced-code block that runs to EOF; the walker accepts it as CodeBlock with content trailing to end of input. Equivalent recovery for unclosed emphasis, mismatched table rows, etc.
scenarios.scenario-8: Round-trip: markdown_to_spec(md) → build_document_from_spec(spec) → save(out) → MP-VALIDATE.run_checks(out, level="lenient") returns passed=True with hard_count=0. Run on tests/fixtures/sample_article.md as a smoke fixture.
scenarios.scenario-9: REPORT_COMPARISON.md regression: experiments/2026-05-10-alpha-baseline/REPORT_COMPARISON.md → markdown_to_spec → docx must contain at least 3 actual `<w:tbl>` elements (one per source table) AND zero stray `Normal`-styled paragraphs that quote pipe-table cells. This is the explicit fix for the embarrassing 0.4.0a1 baseline_report.docx where the LLM serialized tables as parallel paragraphs.
scenarios.scenario-10: Empty input: markdown_to_spec("") raises MD_ADAPTER_EMPTY_INPUT. markdown_to_spec(" \n\n") raises MD_ADAPTER_NO_USABLE_CONTENT (whitespace-only / no extractable blocks).
scenarios.scenario-11: Logs: every markdown_to_spec call emits exactly one [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] at INFO with payload {token_count, section_count, block_count_by_type, has_title_override}. No additional INFO emissions per node (the walker is silent at sub-block level).

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] — fires once per markdown_to_spec call; payload as in scenario-11

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: markdown_to_spec must NEVER call an LLM, NEVER make a network request, NEVER touch the filesystem. Pure function over a string.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must NOT import from tools/article_experiment/runner — that module is the LLM-driven path. Reuse types from tools/article_experiment/spec only (or move shared types into src/mint_python/spec/ in a follow-up).
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must NOT wrap pandoc, mistune, or any other parser. markdown-it-py is the chosen parser; switching is a deliberate Phase-N decision, not an ad-hoc swap.

fixture.purpose: Round-trip happy path — H1 title, H2/H3 sections, mixed inline emphasis, GFM table, fenced code, blockquote. Used by scenario-8.
GFM table with `:--`/`--:`/`:-:` pipe syntax; verifies the alignment metadata on `attrs` reaches the TableBlock for downstream rendering. Used by scenario-2.
Bold inside italic, emphasis spanning multiple inline tokens, emphasized phrase containing punctuation. Used by scenario-6.
Code fence opened with ``` but never closed; markdown-it-py recovery path emits a CodeBlock running to EOF. Used by scenario-7.

Slice of the actual experiments/2026-05-10-alpha-baseline/REPORT_COMPARISON.md focused on the table-heavy section. Regression guard for the 0.4.0a1 LLM-flattened-table embarrassment. Used by scenario-9.
Empty file. Verifies MD_ADAPTER_EMPTY_INPUT raise-path of scenario-10.
File with only spaces and newlines. Verifies MD_ADAPTER_NO_USABLE_CONTENT path of scenario-10.

key.name: token_count
key.desc: Number of root-level markdown-it tokens consumed (proxy for input size)

key.name: block_count_by_type
key.desc: Histogram: keys ⊆ {paragraph, table, callout, list, code}; values are non-negative ints

key.name: has_title_override
key.desc: True iff title_override kwarg was supplied (vs derived from first H1 / fallback)

trace-contract.forbidden-cross-talk: No INFO/WARNING emissions from prefixes other than MP-MdAdapter inside markdown_to_spec. The walker is silent at sub-block level — no per-token, per-section, or per-block logging. Importing markdown-it-py must NOT pull in noisy library loggers (suppress at module load if needed).

verification-levels.module-level: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_md_adapter.py + ruff + mypy. ~10 unit tests covering scenarios 1-11 against the 7 fixture files. Wall time < 1s. Required before any wave merges.
verification-levels.wave-level: (none — adapter is a pure function with no surface beyond ArticleSpec; integration with MP-DOCUMENT round-trip is asserted at module level via scenario-8 fixture)
verification-levels.phase-level: End-to-end regen of experiments/2026-05-10-alpha-baseline/baseline_report.docx via the adapter (no LLM). Manually inspect that tables render as actual `<w:tbl>` elements, not parallel paragraphs. This is the embarrassment-fix evidence; one-shot manual check, not gated in CI.

field-2.name: input_fixture
field-2.desc: Path to the .md fixture or inline string that triggered failure

field-6.name: suggested-action
field-6.desc: Drop down to markdown_it.tree.SyntaxTreeNode dump for the failing input; check token.type vs walker dispatch table; if mismatch is in attrs (alignment, language) — the issue is metadata extraction, not block dispatch

V-MP-MD-ADAPTER.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure function, no shared state, no IO. Tests run in any order, in parallel. Fixtures are read-only files in tests/fixtures/md_adapter/.

module-checks.check-2: Manual review by user (peer review checkbox)

scenarios.scenario-1: Inventory completeness: docs/mcp-audit.md lists every @mcp.tool registration found in src/mint/mcp_g1.py and src/mint/mcp_g2.py. Each entry has: tool name, current signature (params + return type), one-line behavior description, current state classification (stateful / stateless), elicitation usage (yes / no), recommended disposition (kept / migrate / deprecate).
scenarios.scenario-2: Gap analysis covers the three differentiators settled this session: design-tokens-as-resources (currently absent), versioned templates with cross-model handoff (absent), GRACE-as-audit-trail (partially shipped via MP-GRACE — assess what's exposed via MCP vs only available via SDK), elicitation-driven planning dialogs (absent). Each gap has: status, why it matters, what closing it requires, rough effort.
scenarios.scenario-3: Migration plan has prioritized list of changes with acceptance criteria per change. At minimum: (a) which existing tools migrate to elicitation, (b) which new tools to add (list_templates, get_template, update_template, list_presets, generate_document with elicitation), (c) breaking-change call-out for any client-facing API changes, (d) rough sequencing for Phase-14.
scenarios.scenario-4: Roadmap of new tools includes signatures (param names, types, return shape) consistent with FastMCP + the elicitation pattern (`async def, ctx: Context, await ctx.elicit(...)`). Matches the planned MP-MEMO-POC.create_memo signature as the canonical example.
scenarios.scenario-5: Honest assessment of what cannot be salvaged: any legacy tool whose semantics conflict with the new product shape is explicitly listed for deprecation with a 1-line rationale. Empty list is acceptable if everything migrates cleanly; an empty section requires a "no deprecations" note rather than silence.

required-log-markers.marker-1: (none — design module has no runtime path)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: This module ships NO runtime code. If the audit produces code recommendations, they go in Phase-14 module entries, not here.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Audit must NOT propose merging the legacy mcp_g1 + mcp_g2 into a single new package without first surfacing what's in each — inventory before consolidation.

doc-structure.section-1: Markdown table with columns: tool name, signature (params + return type), one-line behavior description, current state classification (stateful / stateless), elicitation usage (yes / no), recommended disposition (kept / migrate / deprecate). One row per @mcp.tool registration found across mcp_g1.py + mcp_g2.py. Sorted alphabetically within each source file.
doc-structure.section-2: Narrative paragraph per non-trivial tool group; explicit call-out for any tool reading or writing shared mutable state, any tool whose behavior depends on call ordering, any tool that holds resources across calls (file handles, sockets, in-memory caches).
doc-structure.section-3: Four sub-headings — one per differentiator settled in Phase-13 planning: (a) design-tokens-as-resources, (b) versioned templates with cross-model handoff, (c) GRACE-as-audit-trail, (d) elicitation-driven planning dialogs. Each sub-section: Status (absent / partial / shipped), Why-it-matters (one paragraph), Closing-it-requires (bullet list of work items), Rough-effort (T-shirt size or hour estimate).
doc-structure.section-4: Prioritized list of changes with acceptance criteria per change. Required content: (a) which existing tools migrate to elicitation, (b) which new tools to add (list_templates, get_template, update_template, list_presets, generate_document with elicitation), (c) breaking-change call-out for any client-facing API changes, (d) rough sequencing for Phase-14 (which migrations gate which others).
doc-structure.section-5: Concrete signatures (param names + types + return shape) for the new tools. All async, all take ctx: Context, all use ctx.elicit for missing required arguments. Match the planned MP-MEMO-POC.create_memo signature as the canonical example.
doc-structure.section-6: What cannot be salvaged: any legacy tool whose semantics conflict with the new product shape is explicitly listed for deprecation with a 1-line rationale. Empty list is acceptable, but the section MUST contain an explicit "no deprecations needed" note rather than silence.

verification-levels.module-level: docs/mcp-audit.md exists; grep confirms all 6 H1/H2 section headings; no "TODO" / "TBD" / "..." placeholders in section bodies; tool inventory has at least one row per source file (asserts the file was actually read end-to-end).
verification-levels.phase-level: User peer review checkbox in PR description; user signs off on the migration plan acceptance criteria before Phase-14 kicks off. The audit is the brief that Phase-14 is built against — sign-off here gates the next phase.

field-1.name: missing_section
field-1.desc: Which of the 6 required sections is missing or empty

field-2.name: placeholder_count
field-2.desc: Count of TODO/TBD/... markers found in section bodies

field-3.name: inventory_row_count
field-3.desc: Number of rows in the inventory table; should match @mcp.tool count from grep across mcp_g1.py + mcp_g2.py

field-4.name: suggested-action
field-4.desc: Re-read source mcp_g{1,2}.py for the missing inventory rows; cross-reference each gap against the four differentiator list items in section 3 to confirm coverage

V-MP-MCP-AUDIT.autonomy-assessment: SOLO — design deliverable, requires human peer review by user. Not parallelizable; not safe for autonomous agents to mark "done" without explicit user sign-off.

module-checks.check-4: Manual smoke through Claude Desktop (documented procedure in docs/memo-poc-smoke.md)

scenarios.scenario-1: Full intent: create_memo(intent="Memo from Mikhail (CFO) to Board on 2026-05-15 about Q2 revenue trends. Q2 revenue grew 13%...", source_md=None, ctx=fake_ctx) extracts all 5 fields heuristically; ctx.elicit is NEVER called. Returns {"path": ..., "audit_id": ..., "fields_elicited": []}.
scenarios.scenario-2: Single missing field: intent without an explicit recipient triggers exactly one ctx.elicit call with field name "recipient" and a non-empty prompt message. Test injects a fake ctx that records elicit invocations; asserts call count == 1 and the requested field == "recipient".
scenarios.scenario-3: Multi-round elicitation: intent missing both sender and date triggers two elicit calls in declaration order (sender first, then date). fields_elicited in result == ["sender", "date"].
scenarios.scenario-4: Elicitation rejected: ctx.elicit returns ElicitResult(action="reject") for the first missing field. Tool raises MEMO_ELICITATION_REJECTED with the rejected field name. No docx is produced; no GRACE manifest emitted.
scenarios.scenario-5: Lenient validation: the docx produced by a successful create_memo run passes MP-VALIDATE.run_checks(level="lenient") with hard_count=0.
scenarios.scenario-6: Klawd visual: word/styles.xml in the saved docx contains `1B3A5C` (klawd primary), `Arial`, and `333333` (klawd body). This is the same regression guard pattern as test_klawd_preset_visually_applied_to_saved_styles_xml — mismatch indicates apply_preset_to_doc regressed.
scenarios.scenario-7: GRACE injection: the saved docx has the urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest Custom XML Part. The manifest's instructions list contains a memo-poc-specific entry naming the audit_id, model identity (from ctx if available, else "anonymous"), timestamp, and fields_elicited.
scenarios.scenario-8: Log markers fire in this order per create_memo run: BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT (once), BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD (zero or more, one per missing field), BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX (once), BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE (once). All under [MP-Memo] prefix.
scenarios.scenario-9: Manual Claude Desktop smoke: run the memo-poc-smoke.md procedure end-to-end with a real Claude Desktop client. Expected: form prompts appear in the Claude Desktop UI for missing fields; user-provided values flow back; final docx download link appears in the Claude reply. Failure of any step is documented as a follow-up issue, not a test-suite failure (out of CI's reach).

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Memo][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT] — once per create_memo call; payload: source_md_present, fields_extracted_heuristically
required-log-markers.marker-2: [MP-Memo][elicit][BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD] — zero or more per call; payload: field_name, accepted_or_rejected
required-log-markers.marker-3: [MP-Memo][build][BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX] — once on the success path; payload: output_path, docx_size_bytes
required-log-markers.marker-4: [MP-Memo][grace][BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE] — once on success; payload: audit_id, instructions_count

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: create_memo MUST NOT call an LLM directly — the only LLM interaction is via the connected MCP client through ctx.elicit / ctx.sample. Direct httpx / openai / anthropic invocation is forbidden.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: MUST NOT emit raw OOXML XML in any return value or log. The MCP client never sees XML — only paths, audit ids, and structured field reports.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Memo template structure (which fields, in what order, what blocks) MUST live in templates/memo.yaml, NOT embedded in src/mint_python/mcp/memo.py. This is the "design as data, not code" rule applied to this module.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: MUST NOT introduce a custom session-state convention (start_session / continue_session). All multi-turn dialog goes through ctx.elicit() — the spec-native primitive — so the server composes with elicitation-aware MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Copilot, Cursor, FastMCP-based hosts).

fixture.purpose: All 5 fields embedded inline in plain text — heuristic extractor finds them all, ZERO ctx.elicit calls expected. Used by scenario-1.
4 of 5 fields in intent text, recipient absent — exactly ONE ctx.elicit call for 'recipient' expected. Used by scenario-2.
3 of 5 fields in intent, sender + date absent — TWO ctx.elicit calls in declaration order (sender first, then date). Used by scenario-3.
Realistic chat transcript fed via source_md kwarg; subject extracted from chat topic line, body extracted from chat content. Sanity-checks the MD-ADAPTER → MEMO-POC integration.

Golden MemoTemplate-rendered layout: ordered list of (section_kind, content_pattern) tuples. Used as the assertion reference for scenario-1 — confirms the YAML template loader produced the documented section sequence.

helper.purpose: FakeMCPContext class: drop-in replacement for fastmcp.Context in unit/integration tests. Constructor takes a scripted answer dict (e.g. {'recipient': 'Board of Directors', 'date': '2026-05-15'}). Each ctx.elicit(message, response_type) call records (field, message) into self.elicited_calls (list[tuple[str, str]]) and returns the scripted answer wrapped as ElicitResult(action='accept', data=answer). To trigger reject path, scripted dict carries a sentinel '__REJECT__' value for the rejected field. The helper is the primary evidence source for scenarios 1-4 and 8.
Package marker so the fake_mcp_context import works from tests/integration/.

block-1.name: BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT
block-1.position: first emission of any kind in the trace

block-3.name: BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD
block-3.position: after BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT (and BLOCK_PARSE_MD if present); ordering across multiple emissions follows MemoSpec field declaration order: sender, recipient, date, subject, body

block-4.name: BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX
block-4.condition: success path only — absent if MEMO_ELICITATION_REJECTED was raised

block-5.name: BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX
block-5.position: emitted by MP-DOCUMENT during the save call create_memo invokes

block-6.name: BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET
block-6.position: emitted by MP-STYLE during Document.save preset wiring

block-8.name: BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST
block-8.position: emitted by MP-GRACE during the bootstrap call

key.name: fields_extracted_heuristically
key.desc: Subset of {sender, recipient, date, subject, body} — fields the heuristic populated from intent + source_md without elicitation

key.name: action
key.desc: ElicitResult outcome from the connected MCP client (or FakeMCPContext in tests)

key.name: attempt
key.desc: 1-based; current implementation never retries an elicitation, so always 1. Kept in payload for forward-compat with future retry policies.

key.name: instructions_count
key.desc: Number of GRACE instruction rules in the manifest (memo-poc bootstrap adds at minimum the audit-trail entry to the default instruction set)

forbidden-cross-talk.item-1: NO httpx, openai, anthropic, or other LLM-client logger emissions during create_memo. The only LLM interaction is via ctx.elicit, which goes through the MCP client process — not directly from the tool process.
forbidden-cross-talk.item-2: NO [Sandbox] or [JS-Engine] markers. create_memo is pure Python through MP-DOCUMENT; the legacy js engine is not on this path.
forbidden-cross-talk.item-3: NO [Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] emissions inside create_memo. Validation is a separate step the caller performs after create_memo returns; bundling it inside the tool would couple the MCP-tool surface to a second concern.

trace-contract.on-rejection-trace: When MEMO_ELICITATION_REJECTED is raised: the trace ends after the rejecting BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD with action="reject". BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX, BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX, BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET, BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE, and BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST MUST NOT appear in the trace. Verifies via caplog assertion that only the prefix-set {MP-Memo, MP-MdAdapter} appears in INFO records up to the rejection point, and nothing after.

V-MP-MEMO-POC.cross-module-evidence:
Module-level evidence chain for the success path (fixture: intent_missing_recipient.txt with FakeMCPContext({'recipient': 'Board of Directors'})):
[MP-Memo][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT] └→ [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] (only if source_md present) [MP-Memo][elicit][BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD] field=recipient action=accept [MP-Memo][build][BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX] └→ [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] └→ [MP-Style][apply_preset_to_doc][BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET] [MP-Memo][grace][BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE] └→ [MP-Grace][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST]
caplog-based scenarios verify the chain holds. A break in the chain (e.g. BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET missing) signals a regression in the intermediate module — the failure packet's `trace_break_at` field names the FIRST missing block to localize the divergent module without re-running the whole stack.
Diagnostic mapping for missing blocks: BLOCK_PARSE_MD missing → source_md handling in create_memo skipped MP-MD-ADAPTER call BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX missing → elicitation completed but Document assembly failed silently BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX missing → Document.save() not called inside build BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET missing → Document.save() didn't invoke apply_preset_to_doc (regression in MP-DOCUMENT or MP-STYLE) BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE missing → success-path skipped GRACE injection (audit-trail broken) BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST missing → MP-GRACE.bootstrap() not actually called from create_memo

verification-levels.module-level: pytest tests/integration/test_mp_memo_poc.py with FakeMCPContext + ruff + mypy. ~8 scenarios (1-8 deterministic + integration), wall time 3-5s. Covers full intent / partial intent / multi-round elicit / rejection / lenient validation / klawd visual / GRACE injection / log marker presence and ordering. Required gate before any wave merges.
verification-levels.wave-level: End-to-end through a stdio FastMCP harness (FastMCP test fixture; no real MCP client). FakeMCPContext-replay still in use, but the tool is reached through the actual FastMCP transport surface. Asserts: tool registers correctly, accepts JSON-serializable args, returns JSON-serializable result, errors propagate as MCP-protocol error responses (not Python tracebacks). 1-2 scenarios, ~2s wall time.
verification-levels.phase-level: Manual smoke through Claude Desktop per docs/memo-poc-smoke.md (procedure to be authored as part of step-3). Out of CI; documented procedure with screenshots, single-user, single Claude Desktop instance, not parallelizable. Pass criteria: form prompts appear in Claude UI for each missing field; user-provided values flow back to the tool; final response in the chat references the saved docx path / audit_id.

field-2.name: trace_break_at
field-2.desc: Name of the first missing or out-of-order block per the cross-module-evidence diagnostic mapping; localizes the failing intermediate module

field-3.name: elicited_calls_observed
field-3.desc: list[tuple[field, action]] from FakeMCPContext.elicited_calls at the point of failure

field-4.name: elicited_calls_expected
field-4.desc: list[tuple[field, action]] derived from the intent fixture (which fields are absent → which elicitations are expected)

field-5.name: docx_styles_excerpt
field-5.desc: word/styles.xml::Heading1 rPr block (truncated 500 chars) — populated only when scenario-6 fails (klawd visual regression)

field-6.name: grace_manifest_excerpt
field-6.desc: urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest XML excerpt (truncated 500 chars) — populated only when scenario-7 fails

field-7.name: suggested-action
field-7.desc: If trace_break_at = BLOCK_APPLY_PRESET — check Document.save() actually invokes apply_preset_to_doc for the configured preset; if BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST — check MP-GRACE.bootstrap result is not None and writes the namespace; if elicited-calls mismatch — check the heuristic field-extractor in create_memo against the intent fixture text (a regex or token-sequence change in the extractor would cause field-detection drift); if scenario-9 manual smoke fails — re-read the user-provided Claude Desktop log, check for elicit-related errors in stderr

V-MP-MEMO-POC.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-CAREFUL — integration tests use FakeMCPContext (deterministic) and are parallel-safe within tests/integration/test_mp_memo_poc.py. The wave-level FastMCP-stdio harness uses an isolated subprocess per test; safe for parallel runs. The manual smoke through Claude Desktop is single-user, single-instance, NOT parallelizable, and explicitly out of CI scope.

scenarios.scenario-1: create_document(intent, doc_type='memo') reproduces V-MP-MEMO-POC scenario-1 byte-for-byte (same docx structure, same klawd visual, same GRACE manifest schema). Backwards-compat baseline. test_scenario_1_create_document_memo_parity_with_memo_poc.
scenarios.scenario-2: Legacy create_memo alias delegates to create_document(doc_type='memo'); existing FakeMCPContext-driven tests in test_mp_memo_poc.py continue to pass without edits during the transition window. Verified empirically: 43/43 V-MP-MEMO-POC scenarios still green. Alias path keeps emitting [MP-Memo] log markers per test_scenario_2_create_memo_alias_keeps_mp_memo_log_prefix.
scenarios.scenario-3: create_document(doc_type='nonexistent') raises DOC_TYPE_NOT_FOUND with the available doc_types listed in the message. test_scenario_3_unknown_doc_type_raises_with_available_list.
scenarios.scenario-4: create_document(doc_type='letter') uses templates/letter.yaml — different required_fields drive different elicitation order, different layout, klawd preset still applied. Promoted to done in W2 alongside the templates/letter.yaml commit. test_scenario_4_letter_doc_type_uses_real_letter_yaml.
scenarios.scenario-5: [MP-Doc][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT] carries doc_type and template_version in the payload. Cross-module evidence chain otherwise unchanged. test_scenario_5_parse_intent_log_carries_doc_type_and_template_version.
scenarios.scenario-6: structured_content shape extends MEMO-POC's with doc_type and template_version fields. Existing consumers reading {status, path, audit_id, fields_elicited} still work. test_scenario_6_structured_content_extends_with_doc_type_fields.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: create_document MUST NOT contain per-doc_type if/else branches. Specialization comes ONLY from templates/<doc_type>.yaml.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: The legacy create_memo alias MUST keep working until at least one Claude Desktop session has been confirmed broken-free against it on a real conversation.

V-MP-DOC-GENERIC.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-CAREFUL — refactor of an existing tested module; high regression risk if the alias breaks. Module-local tests must include a parity-with-MEMO-POC baseline before scope expands.

scenarios.scenario-1: list_templates returns at least two entries (memo + letter) after templates/letter.yaml lands; each entry carries name, version, doc_type, description, last_modified. test_scenario_1_registry_lists_memo_and_letter + test_list_templates_tool_returns_summary_dicts.
scenarios.scenario-2: get_template('memo') returns the parsed YAML as a dict matching templates/memo.yaml content; required_fields tuple matches MEMO_REQUIRED_FIELDS. test_scenario_2_get_memo_matches_yaml_and_required_fields.
scenarios.scenario-3: get_template('nonexistent') raises TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND naming the available templates. test_scenario_3_unknown_template_raises_with_available_list + test_get_template_tool_unknown_name_raises.
scenarios.scenario-4: A malformed YAML in templates/ surfaces as TEMPLATE_INVALID_SCHEMA at registry-load time, not at create_document call-time. Fail-fast. test_scenario_4_malformed_template_fails_at_load_not_at_use + variants (b non-mapping, c wrong field types, d non-string name).
scenarios.scenario-5: [MP-Templates][load][BLOCK_LOAD_REGISTRY] fires once at registry init with payload {templates_count, doc_types, total_versions}. test_scenario_5_load_registry_log_marker.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Registry MUST NOT mutate templates/ on read paths. Write side lives in MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.

V-MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — read-only over a known directory; no shared state; tests use tmp_path-isolated registry instances.

scenarios.scenario-1: update_template('memo', new_yaml, author='Claude') writes templates/memo_v1.1.yaml (semver bump from existing 1.0); returns {name, version, audit_id, predecessor_version}; templates/_audit.jsonl gains one entry. test_scenario_1_update_writes_versioned_sibling_and_audit_entry.
scenarios.scenario-2: update_template without author elicits author via ctx.elicit; on decline raises TemplateAuthorRequired naming the missing field. Variants test the elicit-success path, the decline path, and the unsupported-elicitation fallback. test_scenario_2_missing_author_elicits_then_writes + test_scenario_2_b_decline_raises_author_required + test_scenario_2_c_unsupported_elicit_raises_author_required.
scenarios.scenario-3: After update, list_templates surfaces both memo_v1.0 and memo_v1.1; get_template('memo', version='latest') returns 1.1; older versions remain accessible. test_scenario_3_versions_coexist_and_latest_resolves_to_bumped + MCP-tool variant test_scenario_3_b_mcp_tools_see_new_version_after_update.
scenarios.scenario-4: create_document with the new template version produces a docx whose GRACE manifest names {template, template_version, template_author} — lineage embedded in audit-trail. test_scenario_4_grace_manifest_carries_template_author_lineage.
scenarios.scenario-5: Cross-model handoff verified live 2026-05-10. Step 1: Claude Desktop authored memo v1.1 via update_template adding a Confidentiality callout (audit_id 104f3643-...; templates/memo_v1.1.yaml + _audit.jsonl entry on disk, author=Claude-Opus-4.7). Step 2: separate chat invoked create_document(doc_type='memo'); _load_template auto-resolved to v1.1; produced docx GRACE manifest carries audit_id=2886278b-..., template=memo.yaml, template_version=1.1, template_author=Claude-Opus-4.7. Handoff went through filesystem + _audit.jsonl, not chat history. Two side-findings logged separately (kind: callout in template layout silently skipped by builder; Jinja default-filter not supported in _substitute) — non-blocking; audit chain itself works end-to-end. One UX paper-cut: Claude Desktop's model paraphrases the file:// link out of the tool result text so the file isn't clickable in chat — file is openable from ~/Documents/MINT/ directly.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: update_template MUST NEVER overwrite an existing version file. Conflicts surface as TEMPLATE_VERSION_CONFLICT.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Audit log MUST be append-only.

V-MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-CAREFUL — writes to a shared directory; tests use tmp_path-isolated MINT_TEMPLATES_DIR override; concurrent-write safety documented but not enforced (single-process MCP server assumption).

scenarios.scenario-1: _list_mint_resources (the enumerator backing resources/list-style listing) returns mint://template/memo, mint://template/letter, mint://preset/klawd, mint://preset/claret_serif, mint://preset/alga_corporate, mint://preset/minimal, mint://preset/compact. Each entry has uri, name, mimeType, description. test_scenario_1_list_mint_resources_enumerates_templates_and_presets.
scenarios.scenario-2: _template_resource_content('memo') returns the latest version's YAML serialization. The @server.resource templated handler is callable in tests directly and returns the same content. test_scenario_2_template_resource_returns_yaml_content + test_scenario_2_b_template_resource_handler_callable_directly.
scenarios.scenario-3: list_presets / get_preset tools project the same data as the resource enumerator/handler. JSON presets get application/json mimeType; YAML presets get application/x-yaml. test_scenario_3_list_presets_tool_matches_resource_listing + test_scenario_3_b_get_preset_tool_returns_content_and_mime.
scenarios.scenario-4: _template_resource_content('nonexistent') and _preset_resource_content('nonexistent') both raise RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND with the available alternatives listed. The get_preset tool surfaces the same error class for cross-surface consistency. test_scenario_4_unknown_template_raises_resource_not_found + variants.
scenarios.scenario-5: _parse_uri rejects malformed inputs (wrong scheme, wrong protocol, missing slash, empty name, path traversal) with RESOURCE_URI_INVALID; message names accepted forms. test_scenario_5_malformed_uri_lists_supported_schemes.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Resource handlers MUST NOT expose anything outside templates/ and presets/ — no general filesystem read.

V-MP-MCP-RESOURCES.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — read-only resource handlers; no shared state.

scenarios.scenario-1: MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS="alice,bob,Claude-Opus-4.7" set; require_template_writer("alice") returns None (no raise). update_template("memo", new_yaml, author="alice") writes templates/memo_v1.2.yaml + appends _audit.jsonl entry per existing W3 path. test_scenario_1_env_allowlist_admits_writer_and_writes_proceed.
scenarios.scenario-2: MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS="alice"; update_template("memo", new_yaml, author="cursor-sidecar") raises TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN before any disk write. templates/_audit.jsonl line count unchanged; templates/ directory listing unchanged. test_scenario_2_unallowed_author_raises_before_disk_io.
scenarios.scenario-3: No MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS set, no ~/.config/mint/writers.json present → open mode. require_template_writer("anyone") returns None; first call emits [MP-Auth][check_writer][BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE] WARNING exactly once per process; subsequent open-mode calls do not re-emit. test_scenario_3_open_mode_warns_once_then_admits.
scenarios.scenario-4: ~/.config/mint/writers.json contains malformed JSON → load_writers_config raises AUTH_CONFIG_INVALID at first call (lazy + cached after success); naming the path and the parse error in the message. NOT raised at module import time. Wave-15-1 worker split into 4a (malformed JSON) + 4b (top-level type) + 4c (missing 'writers' key) + 4d (wrong element type) — separate scenarios so future regressions on schema enforcement are caught individually rather than collapsing into a single test. test_scenario_4_malformed_writers_json_fails_fast_with_path_in_message + 4b/4c/4d variants.
scenarios.scenario-5b: Whitespace-only env value (MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS=" , ") falls through to file/none — open mode applies, NOT a lockout. Documented contract: a misconfigured env doesn't lock everyone out (deployment safety). test_scenario_5b_whitespace_only_env_falls_through.
scenarios.scenario-5c: Whitespace around env entries trimmed: MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS=" alice , bob " admits both alice and bob. test_scenario_5c_env_entries_whitespace_trimmed.
scenarios.scenario-6: VF-017 inv-3 READ-PATH-NEVER-CALLS-AUTH: monkeypatch require_template_writer with a sentinel raising AssertionError; calling list_templates() and get_template("memo") never trips the sentinel. Verifies the read-path-open invariant from the consumer side. test_vf_017_inv_3_read_path_never_calls_auth.
scenarios.scenario-7: VF-017 forbidden-3 WRITER-LIST-NEVER-LOGGED: inject sentinel writer "WRITER_SENTINEL_42" into MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS; trigger admit + deny; assert sentinel never appears in any caplog record getMessage(). test_vf_017_forbidden_3_writer_list_never_logged.
scenarios.scenario-5: [MP-Auth][check_writer][BLOCK_AUTH_DENY] payload on the deny path carries author=<rejected_id> reason="not_in_writers" config_source="env"|"file" — enough information to debug allowlist mismatches without leaking the full writer list. test_scenario_5_deny_log_carries_author_and_config_source.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Read-path tools (list_templates, get_template, list_presets, get_preset, mint_read_grace_manifest, mint:// resource handlers) MUST NOT call into MP-AUTH-SHIM — read remains open by design.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: The shim MUST NEVER write or mutate templates/, _audit.jsonl, or any disk state. Pure decision module.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: WritersConfig MUST NEVER be logged in full — only config_source surfaces in evidence; the writer list itself stays out of logs.

V-MP-AUTH-SHIM.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure decision module with no shared mutable state; tests use monkeypatch on env + tmp_path-isolated config files; load_writers_config cache cleared per test via fixture.

scenarios.scenario-1: create_document(doc_type='memo', ...) produces docx; mint_read_grace_manifest(path) returns dict with audit_id (UUID4 string), fields_elicited (list), template ('memo.yaml'), template_version (semver str), template_author (when post-W3 template was used), timestamp (ISO8601), namespace ('urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest'). Round-trip: dict == _canonicalize(MP-GRACE.describe(path)). test_scenario_1_round_trip_create_then_read_manifest.
scenarios.scenario-2: Docx with multiple grace/manifest_*.xml parts (W3 append-extension scenario where template lineage was added on top of an earlier manifest) → return entry with the highest timestamp; older entries NOT surfaced (Phase-15 returns only most-recent). Worker note: bootstrap currently OVERWRITES rather than appending in a second call; Wave-15-1 test fixture used zipfile.ZipFile(..., 'a') ONLY in test setup to plant additional parts. The unit-under-test never opens in append mode (forbidden-1 invariant verified separately). test_scenario_2_multiple_manifest_parts_returns_most_recent.
scenarios.scenario-3: Docx with NO urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest custom XML part → MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND structured error (NOT a stack trace). Message names the document_path. Verified on a docx produced by a non-MINT pipeline (e.g. python-docx newDocument().save()). test_scenario_3_no_grace_part_raises_manifest_not_found.
scenarios.scenario-4: Path is not a valid zip / corrupted docx → INVALID_DOCUMENT. test_scenario_4_a_invalid_zip_raises_invalid_document. Wave-15-1 worker split out scenario-4-a2 (missing-file branch via not resolved.is_file()) — distinct INVALID_DOCUMENT branch from BadZipFile case; bumped coverage to 100%. Path traversal attempt (e.g. "../../etc/passwd") rejected by safe_doc → INVALID_DOCUMENT before any zipfile open. test_scenario_4_b_path_traversal_rejected. test_scenario_4_a2_missing_file_raises_invalid_document.
scenarios.scenario-5: manifest_*.xml part exists but XML is malformed (truncated, mismatched tags) → MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR with the offending xml_part_name in the message; document_path also surfaced. test_scenario_5_malformed_manifest_xml_raises_parse_error.
scenarios.scenario-6: [MP-Manifest][read][BLOCK_READ_MANIFEST] payload carries document_path + manifest_count + selected_xml_part_name on success; [MP-Manifest][read][BLOCK_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND] fires on the not-found path. test_scenario_6_log_markers_on_success_and_not_found.
scenarios.scenario-7: VF-018 inv-2 READ-ONLY: sha256(docx_bytes) before mint_read_grace_manifest call equals sha256 after, across all 6 V-MP-MANIFEST-READ scenarios (success + not-found + parse-error + invalid-document). Verified via zip_byte_snapshot fixture. test_vf_018_inv_2_read_only_byte_equality.
scenarios.scenario-8: VF-018 forbidden-1 ZIP-MODE-PINNED: zipfile.ZipFile.__init__ patched with sentinel asserting mode in {'r', None (defaults to 'r')}. Run all 6 scenarios; sentinel never trips. test_vf_018_forbidden_1_zip_open_mode_is_read_only.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: mint_read_grace_manifest MUST NEVER mutate the source docx. Read-only by contract — no zipfile.ZipFile open in 'a' or 'w' mode anywhere in the path.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: The tool MUST NOT consult MP-AUTH-SHIM — read is open per V-MP-AUTH-SHIM forbidden-1.

V-MP-MANIFEST-READ.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure read over local file; tests use tmp_path docx fixtures; safe_doc enforces path scope.

scenarios.scenario-1: Backend stack available (soffice + pdftoppm + Ollama VLM mocked through tests/_helpers/fake_vlm fake_vlm_urlopen("above_threshold")). create_document(doc_type='memo') → tool result structured_content carries visual_qa: {score: int, axes: dict[str, int], issues: list, pages_scored: int, preset_name: 'klawd', advisory: True}. score is the mean of axis means across pages. test_scenario_1_hook_attaches_visual_qa_to_structured_content.
scenarios.scenario-2: Fake VLM returns scores averaging <70 (below default threshold). Tool result is still success=True; visual_qa.advisory remains True; [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_BELOW_THRESHOLD] WARNING fires with payload {score, threshold, preset_name}. The tool MUST NOT raise. test_scenario_2_below_threshold_logs_warning_but_tool_succeeds.
scenarios.scenario-3: MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1 set; create_document tool result structured_content does NOT include the visual_qa key at all (key absent, not None). No QA log markers fire. Backend probes are never executed (verified via shutil.which sentinel). test_scenario_3_skip_env_omits_visual_qa_key_entirely.
scenarios.scenario-4: Backend unavailable variants: (a) soffice missing on PATH, (b) pdftoppm missing on PATH, (c) Ollama endpoint unreachable. Wave-15-2 implementation refinement: score_document returns a VisualQAReport with skipped=True (NOT None) so structured_content surfaces visual_qa: {skipped: true, skip_reason: <backend>_unavailable | render_failed:<Exc> | no_pages_rendered | ollama_unreachable:<Exc>, advisory: true} — distinct from the env-skip path (None → key omitted). Backend-probe order: shutil.which(soffice) → shutil.which(pdftoppm) → only then attempt rendering → only then attempt VLM call (no up-front Ollama probe). [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE] WARNING with reason. Tool result success=True. test_scenario_4_a_soffice_missing + test_scenario_4_b_pdftoppm_missing + test_scenario_4_c_ollama_unreachable.
scenarios.scenario-5: Successful score path emits [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_SCORE] payload {preset, pages_scored, score, axes}. Cross-module evidence chain in DF-019: BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE (document.py:910 marker) → BLOCK_QA_SCORE — visual QA always runs AFTER GRACE injection, never before. (Marker-correction Wave-15-2: position invariant pins to BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE, the document.py-level marker; BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST is the inner MP-GRACE marker that fires earlier.) test_scenario_5_block_qa_score_payload_and_position_after_grace.
scenarios.scenario-6: Hook is invariant-locked to advisory mode: when the VLM returns unparseable JSON content (transport succeeded, content-layer broken), score_document logs the failure and returns a VisualQAReport with axes=0 + an issue line containing the unparseable response head — it MUST NEVER raise back into the tool path. Wave-15-2 chose skipped=False with axes=0 over skipped=True because transport succeeded; ops triage benefits from the captured raw head. test_scenario_6_vlm_response_garbage_returns_none_does_not_raise.
scenarios.scenario-7: Defense-in-depth on the never-raise contract: even if a regression makes score_document violate its contract by raising, the hook caller in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py wraps the call in try/except Exception → log warning + degrade. Tool result success=True. Verified by patching score_document to deliberately raise; create_document still succeeds. test_hook_outer_defense_in_depth_swallows_unexpected_score_raise.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Visual-QA outcome MUST NEVER prevent the docx from being returned to the caller. No raise path back into create_document. The hook is advisory by contract.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Hook MUST NOT mutate the saved docx. Read-only inspection over a temp PDF + temp PNGs; tempfiles cleaned up unconditionally (mirrors VF-016 TEMP-FILE-CLEANUP).
