forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: VLM payload MUST NOT include the docx content in clear (only the rendered PNG pages); rubric prompt is fixed and contains no caller-supplied text.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Hook MUST run AFTER MP-GRACE.bootstrap so the GRACE manifest is intact in the visually-scored file.

V-MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-CAREFUL — depends on external binaries (soffice, pdftoppm) and an Ollama endpoint; CI uses a fake-VLM fixture (tests/_helpers/fake_vlm) plus monkeypatched shutil.which for backend-probe scenarios. Real-VLM smoke is manual, single-user, NOT in CI scope.

module-checks.check-2: pytest tests/ -x --timeout=120 # full regression after deletion

scenarios.scenario-1: import mint.mcp_g1 raises ModuleNotFoundError; same for mint.mcp_g2, mint.sandbox, mint.create, mint.assemble. test_scenario_1_legacy_modules_absent (5 ModuleNotFoundError assertions; one per deleted module).
scenarios.scenario-2: mint --engine js validate path/to/file.docx exits non-zero with a deprecation message naming the mint_validate Python successor. The Engine.JS enum value either removed entirely from src/mint/config.py or kept as a deprecation stub that raises CONFIG_ENGINE_REMOVED on load_config (decided at implementation time; either path satisfies the scenario as long as the legacy code path no longer executes). test_scenario_2_engine_js_no_longer_dispatches.
scenarios.scenario-3: pyproject.toml [project].dependencies + [project.optional-dependencies] contain none of: docx, pptxgenjs, exceljs, vm2, isolated-vm, vitest, eslint. uv.lock regenerated; no entries for these packages. pyproject.toml [project.scripts] contains no entries pointing at deleted modules (mint.create / mint.assemble / mint.mcp_g1 / mint.mcp_g2). docs/technology.xml Dependencies block reflects removal. docs/requirements.xml constraint-7 updated to reflect that the JS path is removed entirely (no longer "preserved as optional fallback"). test_scenario_3_a_pyproject_no_legacy_deps + test_scenario_3_b_technology_xml_reflects_removal + test_scenario_3_c_requirements_xml_constraint7_updated + test_scenario_3_d_pyproject_no_dangling_entry_points.
scenarios.scenario-4: Pre-Phase-15 test suite minus tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py + tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py + tests/unit/test_sandbox.py + any test_create*.py / test_assemble*.py = post-Phase-15 test suite. Full pytest run is green; coverage on src/mint_python remains 100% (legacy src/mint coverage is no longer required since the deletions remove uncovered lines wholesale). test_scenario_4_full_suite_green_after_deletion (run as the W3 gate-check rather than a single test).
scenarios.scenario-5: No remaining importer of mint.mcp_g1 / mcp_g2 / sandbox / create / assemble anywhere in the repo. Verified via grep: `grep -rn "from mint\.mcp_g[12]\|import mint\.mcp_g[12]\|from mint\.sandbox\|import mint\.sandbox\|from mint\.create\|import mint\.create\|from mint\.assemble\|import mint\.assemble" src/ tests/ tools/ scripts/` returns zero hits. (Pre-flight 2026-05-10 found 6 hits across mint/create.py + mint/assemble.py + mint/cli.py + tests/; expanded W3 scope deletes all of them.) test_scenario_5_no_dangling_imports.
scenarios.scenario-6: src/mint/cli.py no longer defines a cmd_serve function; `mint serve` subcommand either absent from build_parser OR routes to a stub that exits non-zero with a pointer at the pure-python FastMCP server (src/mint_python/mcp/document.py). test_scenario_6_cmd_serve_removed_or_stubbed.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Phase-15 W3 MUST NOT run before W1 + W2 complete and pass — broad blast radius needs the additive modules stable first.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Deletion MUST be done in a SINGLE PR per Phase-15 commit-protocol — partial deletion (e.g. mcp_g1 deleted, mcp_g2 left) creates a half-broken intermediate state.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: tools/article_experiment/ and any external integration script MUST be audited and migrated (or deleted) BEFORE the legacy modules go away. Memory project_article_experiment confirms it uses bank Ollama directly via Python paths, but a grep is still required.

V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-FORBIDDEN — single-worker only. Subtractive cross-tree edit (src/mint/, pyproject.toml, docs/technology.xml, docs/requirements.xml, tests/integration/test_*) cannot run in parallel with other workers; controller serializes W3 after W1+W2 complete.

Gate-Phase-1.goal: G0 hypothesis validated + G1 deterministic quality gate operational

required-evidence.evidence-1: Sandbox executes docx-js code without violations
required-evidence.evidence-2: All D-H01..D-H10 and P-H01..P-H05 rules have tests with fixtures
required-evidence.evidence-4: StyleFingerprint hash is deterministic for same input
required-evidence.evidence-5: 0 false negatives, <5% false positives on test fixture corpus
required-evidence.evidence-6: All trace markers logged for VF-003 critical flow

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Any model + MINT ≈ Claude document quality (core promise)

required-evidence.evidence-1: Skill prompts auto-selected by tier and format
required-evidence.evidence-2: Template engine fills JSON → valid DOCX/PPTX
required-evidence.evidence-3: Style extraction produces design-tokens.json matching fixture
required-evidence.evidence-4: CreateResult.execution_mode correctly set based on tier
required-evidence.evidence-5: All trace markers logged for VF-001 and VF-002 critical flows

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Visual QA + GRACE metadata + public launch readiness

required-evidence.evidence-1: QA L2 structural checks report confidence and structural findings deterministically; visual overlap detection remains a future render-gate
required-evidence.evidence-2: GRACE metadata readable from injected documents
required-evidence.evidence-3: Cross-model edit test passes (GLM creates → Qwen edits → MINT validates)
required-evidence.evidence-4: All trace markers logged for VF-004, VF-005, VF-006

Gate-Phase-4.goal: Modular generation achieves 90%+ success on 35B models for complex multi-section documents

required-evidence.evidence-1: Plan validation accepts valid plans, rejects invalid (all V-M-PLAN scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-2: Section code generation produces valid JS per section (all V-M-SECTION scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-3: Assembly produces valid DOCX passing L1 validation (all V-M-ASSEMBLE scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-4: Showcase document generated via modular pipeline has >10 paragraphs, >1 table, headings, lists, callouts
required-evidence.evidence-5: Showcase document opens in Word without repair dialog
required-evidence.evidence-6: All trace markers logged for VF-007, VF-008, VF-009 critical flows

Gate-Phase-8.goal: Pure Python Edition Phase 2 (handover §6 Phase 2) lands: Chart factories functional via mint_python.sdk; Section.add_chart unstubbed; chart-bearing 2-section golden document passes M-VALIDATE on lenient mode; matplotlib pinned in pyproject. Phase-7 (chart-free) golden doc remains green — non-regression guard. Foundation ready for Phase-9 (MP-RULES + MP-VALIDATE pure-Python successors).

required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-014 trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold on the e2e chart golden document
required-evidence.evidence-3: M-VALIDATE lenient mode passes on the chart golden document with hard_count=0
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-013 (Phase-7 chart-free golden doc) re-runs green — fingerprint matches mp_e2e_baseline.json
required-evidence.evidence-5: Section.add_chart is concrete — V-MP-SECTION post-Phase-8 scenarios verify positive path; legacy "raises NotImplementedError" scenarios retired
required-evidence.evidence-6: MP-SDK exports Chart per handover §3.4 — V-MP-SDK scenario extends to cover
required-evidence.evidence-7: matplotlib in pyproject.toml [project].dependencies (≥3.7,<4); seaborn NOT a hard dep (lazy-imported)
required-evidence.evidence-8: 100% coverage maintained on src/mint_python/ — chart.py + e2e test contribute new lines all covered

Gate-Phase-9.goal: Pure Python Edition Phase 3 (handover §6 Phase 3) lands: MP-RULES + MP-VALIDATE + MP-FIX as pure-python successors to M-RULES/M-VALIDATE/M-FIX. Document.validate(level) and Document.fix(strategy) unstubbed. Golden doc passes lenient MP-VALIDATE with hard_count=0; fix pipeline converges on bad fixtures; destructive violations rejected. VF-013 + VF-014 non-regression green. Coverage 100% on all new mint_python modules.

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-RULES / V-MP-VALIDATE / V-MP-FIX scenarios pass (12 + 10 + 7 = 29 planned scenarios)
required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-015 trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold on validate+fix pipeline
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-016: Document.validate/fix return concrete reports; BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD absent from validate/fix paths
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-013 (Phase-7 golden doc) re-runs green — fingerprint matches mp_e2e_baseline.json
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-014 (Phase-8 chart golden doc) re-runs green — fingerprint matches mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json
required-evidence.evidence-6: lenient mode MP-VALIDATE on golden doc: hard_count=0, passed=True
required-evidence.evidence-7: D-H09 raw_newline.docx fix converges within 3 iterations; post-fix D-H09=0
required-evidence.evidence-8: 100% coverage maintained on src/mint_python/ — rules/, validate.py, fix.py contribute all lines covered
required-evidence.evidence-9: Destructive rejection: D-H02 fix attempt raises DestructiveRejectedError without mutating file
required-evidence.evidence-10: uv build wheel ships new rules/, validate.py, fix.py entries alongside existing core/ + sdk/

Gate-Phase-7.goal: Pure Python Edition Phase 1 (handover §6 Phase 1) lands: Document/Section/Table/Style stack works end-to-end via mint_python.sdk; a 3-section golden document passes M-VALIDATE on lenient mode; stubs (inject_grace/validate/fix/to_pdf, Section.add_chart) cleanly point to later phases. Foundation ready for Phase-2 (Chart + matplotlib).

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-STYLE / V-MP-CONTENT / V-MP-TABLE / V-MP-SECTION / V-MP-DOCUMENT / V-MP-SDK scenarios pass
required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-013 trace-sequence + invariants hold on the e2e golden document
required-evidence.evidence-3: M-VALIDATE lenient mode passes on the golden document with zero hard violations; soft baseline captured
required-evidence.evidence-4: Stubs raise NotImplementedError with target-phase string and emit BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD; no stub silently no-ops
required-evidence.evidence-5: docs/technology.xml lists python-docx ≥1.1 and pillow ≥10.x in Dependencies
required-evidence.evidence-6: docs/style-preset-schema.md exists and matches the three built-in presets
required-evidence.evidence-7: uv build wheel ships the populated core/ and sdk/ modules; no regression on Wave-6-1's mint_python skeleton wheel layout
required-evidence.evidence-8: Existing 309 tests + new MP-* tests all green; no test required content edits beyond the new MP-* surface

Gate-Phase-6.goal: Pure Python Edition Phase 0 lands as scaffolding only: skeleton importable, dual-engine flag wired through M-CONFIG and M-CLI, default engine=python fails closed via NotImplementedError, --engine js / MINT_ENGINE=js keeps every legacy DF-001..DF-007 flow green.

commands.command-2: ruff check src/mint/config.py src/mint/cli.py src/mint_python tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py tests/integration/test_cli.py
commands.command-4: uv build && unzip -l dist/*.whl | grep -c mint_python

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-PKG scenarios pass (import smoke, version, wheel layout)
required-evidence.evidence-2: All V-MP-FLAG scenarios pass (default→python, env override, garbage→CONFIG_INVALID, _select_engine branches, CLI overrides env, --help)
required-evidence.evidence-3: tests/integration/test_cli.py passes after _run helper is pinned to --engine js — proof that legacy DF-001..DF-007 are unaffected on the js path
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-011 EngineDispatch trace-sequence holds on both branches (js: BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG → BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE → BLOCK_DISPATCH → cmd_*; python: BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG → BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE → NotImplementedError → non-zero exit)
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-011 forbidden-signals hold: no cmd_*/BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS/etc. fires on the python path; no NotImplementedError on the js path
required-evidence.evidence-6: uv build wheel includes 9 mint_python __init__.py files (1 top-level + 8 subpackages)
required-evidence.evidence-7: No existing test required content edits beyond the test_cli _run helper — proof that Phase 0 is non-invasive to the legacy modules' behavior

Gate-Phase-13.goal: MCP-first product shape lands: deterministic markdown adapter (MD-ADAPTER) closes the embarrassing baseline_report regression; design-only MCP audit (MCP-AUDIT) becomes the brief for Phase-14 migration; Memo POC (MEMO-POC) demonstrates the elicitation-driven planning dialog end-to-end through Claude Desktop. The differentiating bundle (design-tokens / governed templates / GRACE audit / elicitation dialogs) is now demonstrable as a coherent story, not just a slide.

commands.command-3: ruff check src/mint_python/adapters src/mint_python/mcp tests/unit/test_mp_md_adapter.py tests/integration/test_mp_memo_poc.py tests/_helpers
commands.command-5: test -f docs/mcp-audit.md && grep -E '^## [1-6]\. ' docs/mcp-audit.md | wc -l | xargs -I{} test {} -ge 6
commands.command-6: python -c "from tools.article_experiment.spec import parse_spec; from src.mint_python.adapters.markdown import markdown_to_spec; from pathlib import Path; spec = markdown_to_spec(Path('experiments/2026-05-10-alpha-baseline/REPORT_COMPARISON.md').read_text()); print(f'sections={len(spec.sections)}')" — proves MD-ADAPTER round-trip on the embarrassment-fix fixture

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-MD-ADAPTER scenarios 1-11 pass; the 7 fixtures in tests/fixtures/md_adapter/ exist and produce deterministic ArticleSpec outputs.
required-evidence.evidence-2: docs/mcp-audit.md exists with all 6 H2 sections present and non-empty; user has signed off on the migration plan acceptance criteria (peer review checkbox in PR).
required-evidence.evidence-3: All V-MP-MEMO-POC scenarios 1-8 pass against FakeMCPContext (scenario-9 manual smoke is documented as a follow-up evidence; not a CI gate).
required-evidence.evidence-4: The cross-module evidence chain in V-MP-MEMO-POC holds end-to-end on the success path — caplog asserts each block fires once, in order, with the documented payload schema.
required-evidence.evidence-5: Forbidden cross-talk asserted: no httpx / openai / anthropic / [Sandbox] markers during create_memo; no [Validate] inside create_memo (validation is post-tool, separate concern).
required-evidence.evidence-6: Klawd visual fidelity intact: scenario-6 confirms saved docx carries #1B3A5C / Arial / #333333 in styles.xml — the apply_preset_to_doc fix from 0.4.0a2 still reaches the Memo path.
required-evidence.evidence-7: GRACE audit-trail intact: scenario-7 confirms the urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest custom XML part is present and carries audit_id + fields_elicited + timestamp.
required-evidence.evidence-8: tests/_helpers/fake_mcp_context.py exists and is importable from both unit and integration tests; FakeMCPContext.elicited_calls is a list[tuple[field, message]] populated by every elicit call.
required-evidence.evidence-9: The embarrassment-fix evidence (command-6 above) produces a docx whose word/document.xml contains at least 3 actual `<w:tbl>` elements when run on the REPORT_COMPARISON.md excerpt — the regression that motivated this entire phase is closed.
required-evidence.evidence-10: No test required content edits beyond the new MP-* surface — proof that Phase-13 doesn't regress anything in Phase-1..12.

Gate-Phase-15.goal: Production hardening lands: write-path tools authorized, GRACE manifests round-trip via mint_read_grace_manifest, every create_document call emits an advisory visual-QA score, legacy mcp_g1/mcp_g2 surface and the JS sandbox path are gone. After Phase-15 the public MCP surface is pure-python, the audit's Priority-1 acceptance criterion is closed, the cross-model handoff demo is safe to run in front of a customer (templates can't be silently clobbered by a sidecar), and the Phase-14 unmet promise to remove the JS path is finally honored. End state: O!Bank-demoable.

commands.command-5: uv build # wheel must build cleanly without docx/pptxgenjs/exceljs Node deps
commands.command-6: python -c "import sys; from importlib import import_module; \ for m in ('src.mint.mcp_g1', 'src.mint.mcp_g2', 'src.mint.sandbox'): \ try: import_module(m); print(f'FAIL: {m} still importable'); sys.exit(1) \ except ModuleNotFoundError: pass; \ print('OK: legacy modules absent')" # gate proof for V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION s1
commands.command-7: grep -rn "from src\.mint\.mcp_g[12]\|import src\.mint\.mcp_g[12]\|from src\.mint\.sandbox\|import src\.mint\.sandbox" src/ tests/ tools/ scripts/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l | xargs -I{} test {} -eq 0 # gate proof for V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION s5

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-AUTH-SHIM scenarios 1-5 pass; MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS env round-trips; open-mode warns once per process.
required-evidence.evidence-2: All V-MP-MANIFEST-READ scenarios 1-6 pass; mint_read_grace_manifest round-trips a manifest written by create_document (closes audit Priority-1 acceptance criterion explicitly).
required-evidence.evidence-3: All V-MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK scenarios 1-6 pass; structured_content gains visual_qa key when hook runs; MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1 omits the key entirely; backend-unavailable paths degrade gracefully.
required-evidence.evidence-4: All V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION scenarios 1-5 pass; ModuleNotFoundError for src.mint.mcp_g1 / mcp_g2 / sandbox; pyproject.toml has no docx/pptxgenjs/exceljs/vm2/isolated-vm/vitest/eslint entries; docs/technology.xml + docs/requirements.xml constraint-7 reflect removal.
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-017 (AuthorizedTemplateWrite) trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold: NO-DISK-WRITE-ON-DENY (inv-1) verified via templates/ + _audit.jsonl byte-equality across deny scenarios; OPEN-MODE-WARNS-ONCE (inv-2) verified via marker_counter == 1 across N=5 admit calls; READ-PATH-NEVER-CALLS-AUTH (inv-3) verified via require_template_writer sentinel patch + read-tool exercise; CACHE-INVARIANT (inv-4) verified via writers.json read-count snapshot; AUTH-CONFIG-FAIL-FAST (inv-5) + WRITER-LIST-NEVER-LOGGED (inv-6) covered.
required-evidence.evidence-6: VF-018 (GraceManifestRoundTrip) trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold: ROUND-TRIP-EQUALITY (inv-1) verified end-to-end via create_document → mint_read_grace_manifest; READ-ONLY (inv-2) verified via sha256 byte-equality across all V-MP-MANIFEST-READ scenarios; NO-AUTH-CALL (inv-3) verified via VF-017 forbidden-4 mirror; PATH-TRAVERSAL-PRE-ZIP (inv-4) verified via ZipFile.__init__ sentinel; MOST-RECENT-WINS (inv-5) verified via dual-manifest fixture; MANIFEST-PAYLOAD-NOT-LOGGED (inv-6) covered.
required-evidence.evidence-7: VF-019 (VisualQAAdvisoryHook) trace-sequence + 7 invariants hold: ADVISORY-ONLY (inv-1) verified via pytest.raises NEVER firing across all 6 scenarios; POSITION-AFTER-GRACE (inv-2) verified via assert_marker_sequence(["BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST", "BLOCK_QA_SCORE"]); SKIP-ENV-OMITS-KEY (inv-3) verified via "visual_qa" not in structured_content assertion; BACKEND-DEGRADATION (inv-4) covered via 3 backend-unavailable variants (soffice / pdftoppm / Ollama); NO-DOC-MUTATION (inv-5) verified via sha256 snapshot; TEMP-FILE-CLEANUP (inv-6) verified via tempdir snapshot diff; NO-CALLER-INPUT-IN-VLM-PAYLOAD (inv-7) verified via fake_vlm sentinel-injection introspection.
required-evidence.evidence-8: Manual smoke through Claude Desktop: (a) MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS=Claude-Opus-4.7 set; update_template from a different author fails with TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN; (b) mint_read_grace_manifest on a freshly-generated docx returns the expected dict; (c) create_document tool result shows visual_qa.score in structured_content. Documented in docs/phase-15-smoke.md (new manual smoke procedure, mirrors docs/cross-model-handoff-smoke.md).
required-evidence.evidence-9: 100% coverage maintained on src/mint_python/; auth.py + manifest.py + qa/visual.py + the create_document hook integration all covered.
required-evidence.evidence-10: uv build wheel ships the new modules and DROPS the legacy Node deps. Wheel size shrinks measurably; binary size delta noted in PR description.

SwarmExecutionReadiness.summary: Current executable verification is strong for deterministic OOXML, validation, edit, theme, MCP, and CLI adapter paths. Swarm execution is permitted only when workers stay inside declared module write scopes and controller-owned docs are edited serially. Phase-6 engine-dispatch verification is ACTIVE (Wave-6-1 landed 2026-05-09: 7ece0df, 2baab4a, 73ae169, 5cc23b9, 832912e + cleanup ddc678a). Phase-7 (Pure Python Edition Phase 1) is DONE — 6 MP-* modules, 5 sub-waves, VF-013 headline acceptance signal green. Phase-8 (Chart + matplotlib) is DONE — 7 chart factories + from_matplotlib/seaborn + VF-014 headline green. Phase-9 (MP-RULES + MP-VALIDATE + MP-FIX) is PLANNED — 3 modules, 4 waves (Wave-9-1..Wave-9-4), VF-015 + VF-016 headline acceptance signals; verification surface finalized and ready for $grace-multiagent-execute.

target-1.evidence: Pure or read-only deterministic tests; run module-local pytest plus ruff/mypy on touched files.
target-1.commands: pytest tests/unit/test_rules/ tests/unit/test_validate.py tests/unit/test_fingerprint.py tests/unit/test_theme.py tests/unit/test_theme_extract.py -x

target-2.evidence: Round-trip, anchor, tracked-change, comments, backup, validation, and trace-order tests are broad. File-mutating tests MUST operate on tmp_path copies only.

target-3.evidence: Current L2 evidence is structural ZIP analysis only. Do not assign visual-render or overlap claims to autonomous workers until a Gotenberg-backed VF and harness exist.

target-4.evidence: Behavior tests exist, but required trace markers are incomplete for sandbox validation/execution and assembly template rendering. Security-sensitive edits require reviewer inspection before merge.

target-5.evidence: tests/unit/test_llm.py is absent. Add deterministic httpx transport tests and confirm [LLM][call][BLOCK_CALL_MODEL] redaction before autonomous work.

target-6.evidence: Phase-6 engine-dispatch contract is implemented and verified end-to-end. Wave-6-1 landed under commits 7ece0df (MP-PKG), 2baab4a (M-CONFIG), 73ae169 (M-CLI), 5cc23b9 (infra), 832912e (V-MP-FLAG tests). 309 unit/integration tests + 1 skipped pass; ruff and mypy clean across the touched surface; uv build wheel includes 9 mint_python __init__.py entries.

target-9.evidence: Phase-9 modules: MP-RULES is a leaf utility — no MP-* deps, read-only shared rules/*.yaml, deterministic XPath evaluation. MP-VALIDATE depends on MP-RULES — opens saved .docx (zip+lxml), deterministic per input XML. MP-FIX depends on MP-VALIDATE — all file mutations target tmp_path copies; backup always created first; destructive rejected; cascade bounded to DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS=3. Wave-9-1 solo, Wave-9-2 solo, Wave-9-3 solo — no parallelism needed (sequential deps). Wave-9-4 solo: in-place unstub of Document.validate/fix + integration e2e. No new hard deps beyond existing pyyaml/lxml/python-docx. VF-013 + VF-014 non-regression re-run at Wave-9-4.
target-9.wave-dependencies: Wave-9-1 unlocks Wave-9-2 (MP-VALIDATE depends on MP-RULES Rule/Violation types); Wave-9-2 unlocks Wave-9-3 (MP-FIX depends on MP-VALIDATE run_checks + SeverityMode). Wave-9-4 solo: unstubs Document.validate/fix + VF-015/VF-016 e2e. Within Phase-9, no parallel waves — each module builds on the previous.
target-9.swarm-fixtures-required: (1) tmp_path + tmp_docx_path for save() targets; (2) caplog_at_info for trace assertions; (3) marker_counter for BLOCK_LOAD_RULES / BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP / BLOCK_APPLY_FIX counts; (4) mp_minimal_config for VF-015 e2e that chains through M-VALIDATE; (5) existing test fixtures at tests/fixtures/ (minimal_valid.docx, bad_column_widths.docx, raw_newline.docx) — read-only access; (6) golden_doc_builder for VF-015 e2e golden doc construction; (7) _mp_helpers helpers for baseline load/write + assert_marker_sequence; (8) MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env gate for baseline rotation.

target-8.evidence: Phase-8 module: matplotlib backend forced to 'Agg' at import (process-local; no display dep); seaborn lazy-imported (optional dep). Wave-8-1 solo (no parallelism within MP-CHART; the 7 factories share file). Wave-8-2 solo: in-place updates to MP-SECTION + MP-SDK + tests + pyproject + uv.lock + new e2e test + new baseline JSON. matplotlib rcParams modifications wrapped in `with mpl.rc_context(...):` to prevent leaks across tests. tmp_path mandate for any image writes. Mid-risk due to matplotlib version variance — pinned matplotlib>=3.7,<4 to stabilize.
target-8.wave-dependencies: Wave-8-1 unlocks Wave-8-2 (Section unstub + SDK re-export depend on Chart class existence).
target-8.swarm-fixtures-required: (1) tmp_path + tmp_docx_path; (2) caplog_at_info; (3) marker_counter for trace counts; (4) mp_minimal_config for in-process M-VALIDATE; (5) MP_CHART_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env gate (parallel to MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE).

target-15.evidence: Phase-15 (production hardening) modules split by execution profile: (a) MP-AUTH-SHIM is leaf utility — pure-decision, stdlib-only, swarm-safe with monkeypatch + tmp_path-isolated config files. (b) MP-MANIFEST-READ is read-only over local docx via MP-GRACE.describe — swarm-safe, parallel with MP-AUTH-SHIM in Wave-15-1 (disjoint write scopes). (c) MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK is single-worker — touches mcp/document.py (high-traffic file) + introduces external-binary deps (soffice, pdftoppm, Ollama); CI uses tests/_helpers/fake_vlm. (d) MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION is SWARM-FORBIDDEN — broad subtractive cross-tree edit (src/mint/, pyproject.toml, docs/technology.xml, docs/requirements.xml, tests/integration/test_*); MUST run last after W1+W2 land green to avoid disrupting in-flight workers.
target-15.wave-dependencies: Wave-15-1 = MP-AUTH-SHIM ‖ MP-MANIFEST-READ (parallel-safe; controller dispatches both workers concurrently). Wave-15-1 unlocks Wave-15-2 = MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK (single worker; touches mcp/document.py — must serialize with any other mcp/document.py edits). Wave-15-2 unlocks Wave-15-3 = MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION (single worker; SWARM-FORBIDDEN; runs ONLY after Wave-15-1 + Wave-15-2 are merged green). W3 forbidden-1: MUST NOT run before W1+W2 complete and pass.
target-15.swarm-fixtures-required: (1) tmp_path + tmp_docx_path for hook tempfile assertions; (2) caplog_at_info for trace assertions on BLOCK_AUTH_*, BLOCK_READ_MANIFEST, BLOCK_QA_*; (3) marker_counter for VF-017 inv-2 OPEN-MODE-WARNS-ONCE (count BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE = 1 across N admit calls); (4) NEW: clean_writers_config fixture clearing the WritersConfig process-cache between tests via reload of mint_python.mcp.auth (mirrors clean_env from test_config.py); (5) NEW: tests/_helpers/fake_vlm fixture providing a deterministic VLM response generator (configurable axis scores, malformed-JSON variant, network-failure variant) — controller-owned helper at tests/_helpers/ alongside the existing fake_mcp_context; (6) NEW: zip_byte_snapshot helper for VF-018 inv-2 READ-ONLY + VF-019 inv-5 NO-DOC-MUTATION; (7) NEW: backend_probe_patcher fixture monkeypatching shutil.which for VF-019 inv-4 BACKEND-DEGRADATION variants; (8) tempdir_snapshot helper for VF-019 inv-6 TEMP-FILE-CLEANUP (snapshot tempdir entries; assert equality before/after).
target-15.controller-pre-flight: Before Wave-15-1 dispatch: provision (a) tests/_helpers/fake_vlm.py with 4 response variants (above-threshold, below-threshold, malformed-json, http-error); (b) tests/integration/conftest.py shim re-exports for clean_writers_config + backend_probe_patcher + zip_byte_snapshot + tempdir_snapshot; (c) ~/.config/mint/writers.json schema doc note in templates/_README.md (the docs/ entry is owner-side; the template README gets a one-paragraph pointer for ops); (d) confirm e2e_qa_visual.py exists at repo root (verified 2026-05-10) — Wave-15-2 worker refactors it into src/mint_python/qa/visual.py without losing behavior. Before Wave-15-3 dispatch: grep tools/article_experiment/ + scripts/ for any `from src\.mint\.mcp_g[12]` or `from src\.mint\.sandbox` import; report findings to the user before deletion proceeds.
target-15.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-CAREFUL with explicit gates. Wave-15-1 is the safest entry point (two independent additive modules with stdlib-only or local-IO scopes). Wave-15-2 introduces external-process dependencies that complicate CI determinism — fake_vlm carries the load there. Wave-15-3 is single-worker SWARM-FORBIDDEN by contract; controller serializes after W1+W2 merge.

target-7.evidence: Phase-7 modules are deterministic and tmp_path-isolated. Wave-7-1 (MP-STYLE) is pure deterministic with read-only registry. Wave-7-2 dispatches MP-CONTENT and MP-TABLE in parallel — disjoint files, both depend only on MP-STYLE, no shared state. Wave-7-3 (MP-SECTION), Wave-7-4 (MP-DOCUMENT) are solo. Wave-7-5 closes with MP-SDK + VF-013 e2e — the e2e harness writes to tmp_path; baseline JSON write is gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env. Each MP-* module is forbidden from introducing a module-level mutable singleton (V-MP-* forbidden-3 across the family).
target-7.commands: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_style.py tests/unit/test_mp_content.py tests/unit/test_mp_table.py tests/unit/test_mp_section.py tests/unit/test_mp_document.py tests/unit/test_mp_sdk.py tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py -x --timeout=120
target-7.wave-dependencies: Wave-7-1 unlocks Wave-7-2; Wave-7-2 unlocks Wave-7-3; Wave-7-3 unlocks Wave-7-4; Wave-7-4 unlocks Wave-7-5. Within Wave-7-2, MP-CONTENT and MP-TABLE are parallel-safe (disjoint write scopes).
target-7.swarm-fixtures-required: (1) tmp_path for every save() and image write; (2) caplog for trace assertions; (3) reuse of central clean_env fixture from tests/unit/test_config.py if MP-* tests touch any config-driven path (most do not); (4) MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env gate for the baseline JSON write — defaults to read-only.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Normalize executable trace markers to source reality before asserting on them: [Skills][select_skill][BLOCK_SELECT_SKILL], [Templates][fill][BLOCK_FILL], [Extract][parse_theme][BLOCK_PARSE_THEME], [Extract][analyze_layouts][BLOCK_ANALYZE_LAYOUTS], [QA][l2][BLOCK_L2_STRUCTURAL], [LLM][call][BLOCK_CALL_MODEL].
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Before sandbox or assembly trace gates can be trusted, add INFO markers for [Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_VALIDATE_CODE], [Sandbox][execute][BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE], and [Assemble][template][BLOCK_ASSEMBLE_TEMPLATE].
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Do not log API keys, prompts, raw OOXML XML, document text, or edit snippets above DEBUG; use op_id, lengths, hashes, and stable IDs at INFO.

level-split.module-level: Run only touched module tests plus ruff/mypy for touched source. Prefer pytest -x and tmp_path fixtures for file-mutating modules.
level-split.wave-level: Run integration checks for surfaces touched by the wave only. For multi-agent waves, the controller owns docs, pyproject, .env.example, and shared test helpers unless the wave explicitly assigns one worker to that file.
level-split.phase-level: Run pytest tests/ -x --timeout=120, ruff check src/mint tests, and mypy src/mint after all wave changes merge. Add src/mint_python to phase commands only after Wave-6-1 activates.

open-gaps.gap-1: Helper modules src/mint/_security.py, src/mint/_xml_ns.py, src/mint/paths.py, and src/mint/sandbox/runner.js lack MODULE_CONTRACT / MODULE_MAP headers.
open-gaps.gap-2: Phase-6 source and tests are still absent in the current checkout; keep V-MP-FLAG and VF-011 marked planned until MINT_ENGINE, Engine, _select_engine, src/mint_python, and tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py exist.
open-gaps.gap-3: M-THEME and M-THEMEEXTRACT are verified and present in the graph, but their placement in development-plan.xml should be refreshed so future agents do not treat them as orphaned modules.

SwarmFixtures.SCOPE: Phase-7 (MP-*) and forward; Phase-8 chart additions documented inline; Phase-9 rules/validate/fix additions documented inline
SwarmFixtures.intent: Per-module subagent execution requires a stable, controller-owned shared-fixture surface. Workers do NOT invent fixtures; they import what is documented here. This block is the contract between the controller (who provisions fixtures) and Wave-7-* workers (who consume them).

ownership.controller-owns: tests/unit/conftest.py and tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py — provisioned BEFORE Wave-7-1 dispatches; updated only by the controller in batched commits, never by per-module workers.
ownership.worker-consumes: Each Wave-7-* worker imports fixtures from conftest.py via pytest's auto-injection AND imports helpers from `tests.unit._mp_helpers` explicitly. Workers MUST NOT redefine these fixtures locally (mirrors V-M-CONFIG forbidden-4 enforcement).
ownership.integration-conftest-shim: Pytest does NOT auto-discover the unit conftest for tests under tests/integration/. The shim re-exports unit fixtures into the integration namespace. Controller-owned: extends ONLY the re-export list when a new integration test needs a fixture not yet shimmed (e.g. Wave-8-2 added chart_baseline_path + mpl_figure_cleanup). Workers MUST stop and report when their integration test consumes a fixture missing from the shim — controller updates the shim in a grace(meta) commit, NOT worker. Considered alternatives: local fixture redefinition (violates SwarmFixtures rule-5); pytest_plugins=[...] (collision-prone when both unit and integration trees collect together); chosen approach is the explicit re-export pattern documented in tests/integration/conftest.py MODULE_CONTRACT.

conftest-spec.note: Phase-7 ships this conftest. It does NOT override `clean_env` from tests/unit/test_config.py — that fixture stays local and continues to be the single chokepoint for MINT_ENGINE / config env scrubbing per V-M-CONFIG forbidden-4. The new conftest adds MP-* specific fixtures alongside.

fixture-1.name: mp_clean_env
fixture-1.purpose: Scrub Phase-7 specific env vars + reset module-level registry state between tests. Complementary to (NOT replacing) clean_env in tests/unit/test_config.py.
fixture-1.registry-reset: If mint_python.sdk has been imported, snapshot mint_python.sdk.presets at fixture entry and restore at teardown — guards against scenario where load_preset(path=...) accidentally mutates the registry (V-MP-STYLE forbidden-3 regression).
fixture-1.implementation-hint: Use `monkeypatch.delenv("MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE", raising=False)` and a try/finally that copies `dict(mint_python.sdk.presets)` if the module is already in sys.modules.

fixture-2.name: tmp_docx_path
fixture-2.implementation-hint: `return tmp_path / "out.docx"` (one-liner; tmp_path is a pytest builtin).

fixture-3.name: mp_minimal_config
fixture-3.purpose: A fully-populated MintConfig with engine=Engine.PYTHON, severity_mode=SeverityMode.LENIENT, and required LLM fields filled with sentinel values. Used by tests that invoke `mint.validate.validate(saved_path, config)` in-process without going through M-CLI.
fixture-3.fields: llm_base_url="http://x:1", llm_model="m", model_tier=Tier.SMALL, severity_mode=SeverityMode.LENIENT, sandbox_timeout=30, engine=Engine.PYTHON, paths set to tmp_path-rooted defaults via tmp_path fixture chain
fixture-3.implementation-hint: Build directly via `MintConfig(...)` constructor; do NOT call load_config() (that reads os.environ which the autouse mp_clean_env scrubbed).

fixture-4.name: caplog_at_info
fixture-4.purpose: Wrapper around pytest's caplog that pre-sets level=logging.INFO so MP-* log markers (which emit at INFO) are captured. Saves every test from repeating `caplog.set_level(logging.INFO)`.

fixture-5.name: marker_counter
fixture-5.purpose: Returns a callable: `count(caplog) -> collections.Counter` mapping BLOCK_NAME → count. Encapsulates the caplog → marker-name extraction so tests assert against a Counter rather than parsing log strings inline.
fixture-5.implementation-hint: `from tests.unit._mp_helpers import extract_marker; return lambda cl: Counter(filter(None, (extract_marker(r.getMessage()) for r in cl.records)))`

fixture-6.name: golden_doc_builder
fixture-6.purpose: Returns the `build_golden_document(out_path)` callable from `_mp_helpers`. Wave-7-5 e2e test uses this; earlier waves do not.

fixture-7.name: schema_violation_factory
fixture-7.purpose: Returns a callable that produces minimally-broken preset JSON dicts targeting specific field paths. Used by V-MP-STYLE scenario-6 (parametrized schema-violation cases). Keeps the test body short and the violation catalog explicit.
fixture-7.implementation-hint: Internal dict template per violation_kind; raises ValueError on unknown kind.

fixture-8.name: mpl_figure_cleanup
fixture-8.purpose: Force-close all matplotlib Figures after each test to prevent memory accumulation under long pytest runs (matplotlib retains Figure refs in pyplot's internal registry until explicit close). Autouse so any test that touches matplotlib (directly or via Chart) benefits without opt-in.
fixture-8.implementation-hint: Try-import matplotlib.pyplot as plt INSIDE the fixture (not at conftest import time — that would force matplotlib import even for tests that don't need it); on ImportError, skip the cleanup (Phase-7 tests don't need matplotlib). Yield, then `plt.close('all')` at teardown if matplotlib was importable.

fixture-9.name: mpl_rcparams_snapshot
fixture-9.purpose: Snapshot matplotlib.rcParams at fixture entry, restore at teardown. Asserts no rcParams leak across test boundaries. Used by V-MP-CHART scenarios that verify corporate theming applies WITHIN a chart construction without polluting global state. Opt-in (NOT autouse) — only tests that exercise theming need it.
fixture-9.implementation-hint: Try-import matplotlib; on ImportError, skip with pytest.skip("matplotlib not installed"). Snapshot via `dict(matplotlib.rcParams)`; yield; assert at teardown that rcParams equals snapshot OR restore via `matplotlib.rcParams.update(snapshot)` and assert. Choice: STRICT (assert equality, fail on leak) — V-MP-CHART forbidden-5 makes this the contract.

fixture-10.name: chart_baseline_path
fixture-10.purpose: Path to tests/fixtures/mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json, parallel to mp_e2e_baseline.json. Encapsulates the path resolution so VF-014 tests don't reach across the repo with `Path(__file__).parents[2] / ...` patterns.
fixture-10.implementation-hint: Returns `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "fixtures" / "mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json"` — symmetric to load_audit_baseline path resolution in _mp_helpers.

helpers-spec.note: Pure functions; NO pytest fixtures live here. The leading underscore signals "test infrastructure" — pytest will not collect this as a test file.

helper-1.name: build_golden_document
helper-1.purpose: Single source of truth for the VF-013 golden document construction (see VF-013 golden-document-spec). Builds via mint_python.sdk; calls Document.save(out_path); returns out_path.
helper-1.reproducibility: This function is the ONLY way Phase-7+ code constructs the golden doc for verification. Inlining the doc-construction in test bodies is forbidden — VF-013 reproducibility invariant.
helper-1.activation-gate: Activated at Wave-7-5 — replaces the pre-Wave-7-5 NotImplementedError stub with the concrete construction. Status: ACTIVE since 2026-05-09 (commit 9793ff4).
helper-1.body-hint: Mirrors VF-013 golden-document-spec/body-spec exactly: alga_corporate preset, cover (Q2 Memo / Phase-7 acceptance fixture), 2 sections (Summary + Details) with paragraph + table content, add_toc(max_level=2), save.

helper-2.name: extract_marker
helper-2.purpose: Parse a log line of the form `[Module][function][BLOCK_NAME] payload` and return BLOCK_NAME, or None if the line doesn't match. Used by marker_counter and assert_marker_sequence helpers.

helper-3.name: assert_marker_sequence
helper-3.signature: assert_marker_sequence(caplog: pytest.LogCaptureFixture, expected: list[str], strict: bool = True) -> None
helper-3.purpose: Assert that the given BLOCK_NAME sequence appears in caplog records in order. strict=True (default) requires the captured marker subsequence to MATCH expected exactly (no other markers interleaved among them); strict=False allows extras between expected items but still asserts ordering.
helper-3.error-shape: On failure, AssertionError message MUST list expected, actual, and the divergence index — so failures are localizable without re-running.
helper-3.usage-hint: Used by VF-013 e2e harness for the trace-sequence assertion: `assert_marker_sequence(caplog, ["BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET", "BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE", "BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE", "BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX"], strict=False)` — strict=False because BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT events interleave naturally.

helper-4.name: assert_no_legacy_markers
helper-4.purpose: Assert that NO log records carry markers from the legacy js engine surface. Used by VF-013 forbidden-5 ("no [Sandbox]/[Validate]/[Edit]/etc. markers from the golden-doc construction path").
helper-4.exception-policy: Tests that EXPLICITLY invoke M-VALIDATE on a saved file (e.g., the e2e harness step that calls `mint.validate.validate(...)`) MUST partition caplog: capture markers up-to-and-including BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX with this helper, then start a fresh caplog scope for the M-VALIDATE invocation. The helper does NOT auto-segment.

helper-5.name: load_audit_baseline
helper-5.purpose: Load `tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json` and return as dict. Raises FileNotFoundError with a clear "set MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1 on the first run" hint if the baseline is missing.
helper-5.baseline-write-protocol: If `os.environ.get("MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE") == "1"`, callers may write the baseline back via `write_audit_baseline(data)`. Both helpers gate on the env var; concurrent xdist workers cannot race-overwrite.

helper-6.name: write_audit_baseline
helper-6.purpose: Write `tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json` ONLY when MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1. Otherwise raises RuntimeError with the rotation protocol from V-MP-DOCUMENT/baseline-update-protocol.

helper-7.name: build_chart_golden_document
helper-7.purpose: VF-014 chart golden document builder — single source of truth for the Phase-8 e2e fixture. Pre-Wave-8-2 STUB raises NotImplementedError naming the activating wave; Wave-8-2 worker replaces the body per VF-014 golden-document-spec (alga_corporate; 2 sections each with 1 paragraph + 1 chart of different type — bar + line; both width_inches=5.0).
helper-7.reproducibility: Mirror the build_golden_document pattern (helper-1) — single function, no inline construction in test bodies. The golden-document-spec is normative.
helper-7.activation-gate: Activated at Wave-8-2 step-5 (test surface). Pre-condition for activation: VF-014 golden-document-spec frozen + MP-CHART (Wave-8-1) shipped + MP-SECTION add_chart unstub (Wave-8-2 step-2) + MP-SDK Chart re-export (Wave-8-2 step-3) all complete. STUB body MUST raise NotImplementedError naming "Wave-8-2" verbatim so a worker that imports this helper before activation gets a clear "wrong wave" signal.

helper-8.name: assert_chart_inline_shape_emu
helper-8.purpose: Open the saved .docx via python-docx, walk doc.inline_shapes, assert each .width.emu == round(expected_widths_inches[i] × 914400). On failure, AssertionError lists actual vs expected per index.
helper-8.usage-hint: Used by VF-014 inv-3 (EMU PRECISION) — verifies python-docx Inches() conversion has no rounding drift across versions.

helper-9.name: assert_seaborn_not_imported
helper-9.purpose: Assert `'seaborn' not in sys.modules`. Used by V-MP-CHART forbidden-3 (seaborn lazy-import contract) — failures indicate someone added a module-level `import seaborn` somewhere in the import graph reaching from `mint_python.core.chart` or test infrastructure.

helper-10.name: load_chart_audit_baseline
helper-10.purpose: Read tests/fixtures/mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json with the same first-run-hint pattern as load_audit_baseline. Raises FileNotFoundError naming MP_CHART_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1 when missing.

helper-11.name: write_chart_audit_baseline
helper-11.purpose: Write tests/fixtures/mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json gated by MP_CHART_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1 — parallel to write_audit_baseline.

helper-12.name: assert_matplotlib_backend_is_agg
helper-12.purpose: Lazy-import matplotlib (or skip if missing); assert `matplotlib.get_backend().lower() == 'agg'`. V-MP-CHART forbidden-2 — fails if a test or module has switched the backend.

V-MP-STYLE.usage: schema_violation_factory drives scenario-6 parametrized case set; caplog_at_info + marker_counter prove BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET fires once per load.

V-MP-CONTENT.usage: marker_counter asserts BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT per .render() call. tmp_path used directly for image fixtures (no dedicated fixture needed; pytest's tmp_path suffices).

V-MP-TABLE.usage: marker_counter asserts BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE counts; deterministic factory tests do not need fixtures beyond the autouse mp_clean_env.

V-MP-SECTION.usage: caplog_at_info captures BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on add_chart stub.

V-MP-DOCUMENT.usage: tmp_docx_path mandatory per forbidden-3 (no stable repo paths). marker_counter asserts BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX count = 1.

V-MP-SDK.usage: Re-export tests need only the autouse fixture; no caplog needed (SDK has no log markers).

VF-013.depends: mp_clean_env, tmp_docx_path, mp_minimal_config, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, golden_doc_builder, plus _mp_helpers.{assert_marker_sequence, assert_no_legacy_markers, load_audit_baseline, write_audit_baseline}
VF-013.usage: Heaviest fixture consumer: builds golden doc via builder, validates with mp_minimal_config, asserts trace via assert_marker_sequence, guards against legacy-engine markers via assert_no_legacy_markers, pins/rotates baseline via load/write helpers gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE.

V-MP-CHART.depends: mp_clean_env, mpl_figure_cleanup (autouse), caplog_at_info, marker_counter, mpl_rcparams_snapshot (opt-in for theming tests), tmp_path, plus _mp_helpers.{assert_seaborn_not_imported, assert_matplotlib_backend_is_agg, extract_marker}
V-MP-CHART.usage: Module-local tests for the 7 factories + from_matplotlib/seaborn/plotly + render. mpl_rcparams_snapshot wraps theming-assertion tests so corporate rcParams modifications don't leak. assert_seaborn_not_imported runs after each from_matplotlib test to confirm lazy-import contract holds. mpl_figure_cleanup is autouse so the worker doesn't have to remember plt.close('all') in every test body.

VF-014.depends: mp_clean_env, mpl_figure_cleanup, tmp_docx_path, mp_minimal_config, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, chart_baseline_path, plus _mp_helpers.{build_chart_golden_document, assert_chart_inline_shape_emu, assert_no_legacy_markers, load_chart_audit_baseline, write_chart_audit_baseline, assert_seaborn_not_imported, assert_matplotlib_backend_is_agg, build_golden_document (for VF-013 non-regression rerun)}
VF-014.usage: Heaviest Phase-8 consumer: builds chart golden doc; asserts trace marker counts (BLOCK_BUILD_CHART=2, BLOCK_RENDER_CHART=2, BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD=0); validates EMU widths via assert_chart_inline_shape_emu; runs M-VALIDATE lenient via mp_minimal_config; pins baseline via load_chart_audit_baseline (gated by MP_CHART_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env); RE-RUNS build_golden_document and asserts its fingerprint still matches mp_e2e_baseline.json — the VF-013 non-regression invariant.

V-MP-RULES.usage: Solo utility module: marker_counter asserts BLOCK_LOAD_RULES and BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE counts. tmp_path for YAML fixture temp files. Read-only access to shared rules/*.yaml.

V-MP-VALIDATE.usage: Depends on MP-RULES (shipped Wave-9-1). marker_counter asserts BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS. tmp_path for save targets. Read-only access to shared rules/*.yaml and tests/fixtures/*.docx.

V-MP-FIX.usage: Depends on MP-VALIDATE (shipped Wave-9-2). marker_counter asserts BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP + BLOCK_APPLY_FIX. tmp_path for save targets and backup files. Read-only access to tests/fixtures/raw_newline.docx.

VF-015.depends: mp_clean_env, tmp_docx_path, mp_minimal_config, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, golden_doc_builder, plus _mp_helpers.{build_golden_document, assert_marker_sequence, assert_no_legacy_markers, load_audit_baseline, write_audit_baseline}
VF-015.usage: Integration e2e: builds golden doc via build_golden_document, validates via MP-VALIDATE, fixes via MP-FIX, asserts baseline fingerprint matches mp_e2e_baseline.json. Also re-runs VF-013 + VF-014 non-regression.

VF-016.depends: mp_clean_env, tmp_docx_path, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, golden_doc_builder, plus _mp_helpers.{build_golden_document, assert_marker_sequence}
VF-016.usage: Document API test: builds doc via SDK, calls doc.validate() + doc.fix() directly, asserts BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD count=0 for validate/fix methods.

SubagentExecutionPackets.SCOPE: Phase-7 Wave-7-* through Phase-9 Wave-9-* per-module dispatch
SubagentExecutionPackets.intent: Each Wave-7-* worker is a fresh, isolated subagent. The controller hands it a packet that is self-contained — the worker does NOT re-read the full GRACE artifacts. This template defines what a per-module packet MUST contain so the worker can implement, verify, and commit without context-switching.

packet-template.field-3: balanced | safe | fast (default balanced; matches development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy).
packet-template.field-4: List of file paths the worker MAY edit. Anything else is OFF-LIMITS — including docs/*.xml, pyproject.toml, .env.example, sibling MP-* sources, tests/unit/conftest.py, tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.
packet-template.field-5: Verbatim copy of the MP-*/contract block from development-plan.xml — purpose, inputs/outputs/errors, interface annotations.
packet-template.field-6: Verbatim copy of the MP-* node from knowledge-graph.xml.
packet-template.field-7: For each module in `<depends>`, a 2-line summary of its public interface (what's safe to import, what raises, log markers it owns). Workers do NOT re-read the dependency's source.
packet-template.field-8: Verbatim copy of the V-MP-* entry from verification-plan.xml — scenarios, log markers, forbidden behaviors, fixture-dependencies.
packet-template.field-9: List of fixtures from SwarmFixtures/conftest-spec the worker MAY consume, with usage notes from fixture-dependency-matrix. Workers MUST NOT redefine these.
packet-template.field-10: List of helper functions from SwarmFixtures/helpers-spec the worker MAY import.
packet-template.field-11: Required log marker format (verbatim from technology.xml log-format). For each marker the module emits, the exact string template + payload fields.
packet-template.field-12: Commands from V-MP-*/module-checks. Worker runs ALL of these before reporting done.
packet-template.field-13: What runs after merge (controller, not worker, runs these — worker is informed so its tests don't duplicate scope).
packet-template.field-14: List of fields the worker MAY claim a delta on (typically just a STATUS flip from planned to done). Workers MUST NOT propose adding new MP-* nodes or new annotations beyond what's pre-listed.
packet-template.field-15: Which V-MP-* scenarios the worker is expected to flip to status="done" after green tests.
packet-template.field-16: Exact commit message template per development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy + the per-wave swarm-policy.
packet-template.field-17: What the worker reports back: changed files, verification commands run + outcomes, graph-delta proposal, verification-delta proposal, commit hash, integration assumptions/surprises.

subagent-isolation-policy.rule-1: Each MP-* module gets a FRESH subagent (no reuse across modules). Workers must not retain state from prior module work.
subagent-isolation-policy.rule-2: Subagent type defaults to `general-purpose` for implementation; `Explore` for read-only research before a complex Wave (e.g. before Wave-7-4 to map python-docx APIs); `Plan` for architecture deltas — but architectural changes MUST route through $grace-plan, not through a worker subagent.
subagent-isolation-policy.rule-3: Per-module subagents are the ONLY sanctioned source of MP-* source-file edits during a wave. Controller code edits (e.g. status flips in docs/*.xml, conftest.py provisioning) are done in the parent context, not via subagents.
subagent-isolation-policy.rule-4: Wave-7-2 dispatches TWO subagents in parallel (MP-CONTENT + MP-TABLE). They MUST NOT cross-import each other's in-progress source — the dependency graph says Section consumes both, but Content and Table are siblings. Their tests MAY both import from MP-STYLE (already shipped in Wave-7-1).
subagent-isolation-policy.rule-5: If a worker discovers it needs a new fixture or helper not in SwarmFixtures, it MUST stop and report — the controller updates SwarmFixtures + provisions the fixture in conftest.py before re-dispatching. Workers NEVER add fixtures locally to test files.

stub-retire-controller-checklist.rationale: Recurring drift pattern detected across Phase-8/9/11 closeouts: when a Phase retires a stub (e.g. Section.add_chart in Phase-8; Document.validate/fix in Phase-9; Document.inject_grace/to_pdf in Phase-11), the closeout commit consistently flips Wave/Phase status flags + V-* scenario states but MISSES the corresponding fn-* annotation on the M-*/MP-* graph node. Result: knowledge-graph.xml describes 'STUB raises NotImplementedError' for an active concrete method. Caught by full-integrity reviewer 3 times already.
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-1: Identify every retired stub in the closing wave. Source-of-truth: grep the source file for the retired method name; confirm the body no longer raises NotImplementedError.
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-2: For each retired stub, locate the matching `<fn-*>` annotation in docs/knowledge-graph.xml under the owning M-*/MP-* module's `<annotations>` block. The PURPOSE attribute typically still says "STUB Phase-N — ..." or "STUB raises NotImplementedError pointing at ...".
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-3: Rewrite the PURPOSE attribute to describe the active surface — keep the delegation target (e.g. "delegates to MP-VALIDATE.run_checks via temp-file save"), the trace markers, and a phase-pointer to the unstub commit (e.g. "Phase-9 unstub commit f021046"). DO NOT keep "STUB" wording unless the surface is genuinely still a stub.
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-4: For each retired stub, locate the matching V-M-* / V-MP-* scenario(s) that described the stub behavior (typically scenario named like "raises NotImplementedError" or "BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on add_X"). Replace with positive scenarios verifying the active surface — see Phase-8 V-MP-SECTION scenarios 5-6 retire/replace as the canonical example.
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-5: If the retired stub was the only inhabitant of a future-module placeholder (e.g. MP-GRACE was registered as STATUS="planned" anchor for Document.inject_grace's delegation target), flip that placeholder module to STATUS="done" with ADDED_AT timestamp + populate `<annotations>` from the actual public surface. See Phase-11 MP-GRACE flip as canonical example.
stub-retire-controller-checklist.item-6: Validate by re-running the controller-sync diff: knowledge-graph.xml MUST show no remaining "STUB" / "STUB Phase-N" / "raises NotImplementedError" wording on retired surfaces (grep `'STUB Phase-' OR 'raises NotImplementedError pointing at'` should yield zero matches except for genuinely-still-stubbed surfaces).

enforcement.rationale: Phase-12 closeout (commit 17da20c) bypassed items 2-6 of this checklist despite the checklist landing in commit ae7a9a7 ten commits prior. Caught by full-integrity reviewer running item-6 grep — checklist works as designed but not enforced. Two paths to make compliance non-optional:
enforcement.option-a: Add a Git pre-commit hook (or CI step) running `grep -nE "STUB Phase-|raises NotImplementedError pointing at" docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/development-plan.xml` and failing the commit when matches exist for already-retired surfaces. Implementation: cross-reference grep output against the active-stubs list maintained in SESSION_HANDOFF — any match outside that list is a fail. Initial active-stubs list is empty post-Phase-12; future stubs added to that list explicitly.
enforcement.option-b: Require every Phase-N closeout commit body to include a literal "stub-retire-checklist: items 1-6 verified" line. Reviewer (full-integrity) treats absence of that line as automatic FAIL when retired stubs are detected.
enforcement.recommendation: Adopt both. Option-a catches the drift mechanically at commit time; option-b creates an audit trail in commit history showing the closeout author actually walked through the checklist.

pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-1: Verify tests/unit/conftest.py is at Phase-8-complete state (10 fixtures). No new fixtures needed for Phase-9 — existing mp_clean_env, tmp_docx_path, mp_minimal_config, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, golden_doc_builder suffice for all 3 modules.
pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-2: Verify tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py contains all 12 helpers through Phase-8. No new helpers needed for Phase-9.
pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-3: Verify rules/d-hard.yaml, rules/d-soft.yaml, rules/p-hard.yaml exist and are readable — MP-RULES reads shared YAML from this directory (cross-package dependency, documented exception to forbidden-2).
pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-4: Verify src/mint_python/rules/__init__.py is the Phase-6 empty skeleton (19 lines, empty MODULE_MAP, no exports) — this is the target file Wave-9-1 replaces.
pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-5: Smoke: `uv run pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -3` confirms test collection count (current baseline + 0 new tests yet).
pre-wave-9-1-controller-checklist.item-6: Single grace(meta) commit: `grace(meta): verify Phase-9 pre-flight — conftest, helpers, rules YAML, skeleton` — push BEFORE Wave-9-1 worker dispatches.

pre-wave-7-1-controller-checklist.item-1: Provision tests/unit/conftest.py with the 7 fixtures defined in SwarmFixtures/conftest-spec. Each fixture body is small (1-5 lines); all bodies live in conftest.py.
pre-wave-7-1-controller-checklist.item-2: Provision tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py with the 6 helpers defined in SwarmFixtures/helpers-spec. Note: helper-1 (build_golden_document) DEPENDS on mint_python.sdk being importable — its body uses lazy import and raises a clear error if mint_python is not yet populated. Implementation only completes after Wave-7-5; Wave-7-1..7-4 workers receive a stub that raises NotImplementedError naming the activating wave.
pre-wave-7-1-controller-checklist.item-3: Run `uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x --collect-only` to verify the new conftest does not break existing test collection.
pre-wave-7-1-controller-checklist.item-4: Commit: `grace(meta): provision tests/unit/conftest.py + _mp_helpers.py for Phase-7 worker fixtures`. Single commit BEFORE dispatching Wave-7-1 worker A.

packet-example.field-4: src/mint_python/core/chart.py, tests/unit/test_mp_chart.py — and ONLY these. matplotlib is pre-installed by the controller (commit BEFORE Wave-8-1 dispatch); chart.py MUST `import matplotlib; matplotlib.use('Agg'); import matplotlib.pyplot as plt` at module top in that exact order. seaborn is NOT imported at module level — only lazy-imported inside Chart.from_seaborn. pyproject.toml is OFF-LIMITS for this worker; tech updates land in Wave-8-2 step-4 (seaborn-optional documentation + uv.lock refresh).
packet-example.field-9: mp_clean_env (autouse), mpl_figure_cleanup (autouse — added Phase-8), caplog_at_info, marker_counter, mpl_rcparams_snapshot (opt-in for theming tests), tmp_path. DO NOT redefine.
packet-example.field-10: extract_marker (used internally by marker_counter); assert_seaborn_not_imported (V-MP-CHART forbidden-3); assert_matplotlib_backend_is_agg (V-MP-CHART forbidden-2).
packet-example.field-11: 3 trace markers per V-MP-CHART required-log-markers: `[MP-Chart][<factory>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] chart_type=%s data_shape=%s` (per factory call); `[MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] chart_type=%s emu_width=%d` (per render); `[MP-Chart][from_plotly][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] method=from_plotly target_phase=Phase 9` (stub touch). Wrap each emit site in `# START_BLOCK_*` / `# END_BLOCK_*` source comments.
packet-example.field-12: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_chart.py -x; ruff check src/mint_python/core/chart.py tests/unit/test_mp_chart.py; mypy src/mint_python/core/chart.py; python -c "import sys; import mint_python.core.chart; assert 'seaborn' not in sys.modules"
packet-example.field-14: knowledge-graph.xml MP-CHART STATUS planned→done (controller flips post-wave). NO new annotations beyond what the Phase-8 plan registered.
packet-example.field-15: V-MP-CHART scenarios 1-16 status planned→done; check-1..3 status planned→done. V-MP-SECTION + V-MP-SDK NOT touched in Wave-8-1 — those updates land in Wave-8-2.
packet-example.field-16: `grace(MP-CHART): add Chart factories + render for Phase-8\n\n... (per development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy commit format)`

packet-example.field-4: src/mint_python/core/style.py, src/mint_python/core/presets/alga_corporate.json, src/mint_python/core/presets/minimal.json, src/mint_python/core/presets/compact.json, tests/unit/test_mp_style.py — and ONLY these. docs/style-preset-schema.md is already shipped (controller-owned).
packet-example.field-9: mp_clean_env (autouse), schema_violation_factory, caplog_at_info, marker_counter — import via pytest auto-injection from tests/unit/conftest.py. Do NOT redefine.
packet-example.field-10: extract_marker (used internally by marker_counter; rarely needed directly).
packet-example.field-11: [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] message format: `"[MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] preset=%s source=%s"` at INFO; payload: preset name + source token ("registry"|"file"). Wrap source emission in `# START_BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET` / `# END_BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET` comments.
packet-example.field-12: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_style.py -x; ruff check src/mint_python/core/style.py src/mint_python/core/presets; mypy src/mint_python/core/style.py
packet-example.field-14: knowledge-graph.xml MP-STYLE STATUS planned→done (controller flips post-wave).
packet-example.field-15: V-MP-STYLE scenarios 1-13 status planned→done; check-1..3 status planned→done.
packet-example.field-16: `grace(MP-STYLE): land Style + Pt + ColorPalette + load_preset for Phase-7\n\n... (per development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy commit format)`

packet-example.field-4: src/mint_python/rules/__init__.py. Replace Phase-6 empty skeleton with concrete Rule/Violation types, YAML loader, XPath evaluate engine, all_rules registry. Rules YAML at rules/ (shared with M-RULES) — read-only, must NOT modify. New test file: tests/unit/test_mp_rules.py. NO edits to any other src/mint_python/* file, NO edits to docs/*.xml, NO edits to tests/unit/conftest.py or tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.
packet-example.field-5: MP-RULES: Pure Python OOXML validation rule definitions loaded from YAML (D-H*, D-S*, P-H*). Successor to M-RULES with MP-* semantic markup. Rule dataclass (id, format, severity, xpath, check, fix_category, hint, description). Violation dataclass. Severity enum (HARD/SOFT). FixCategory enum (SAFE/VISUAL/DESTRUCTIVE). evaluate(rule, tree) → Violation|None. all_rules(rules_dir, doc_format) → list[Rule].
packet-example.field-7: No MP-* production deps — MP-RULES is a leaf utility. Reads YAML from rules/ directory via pyyaml; evaluates XPath via lxml against parsed OOXML XML trees. Rules shared with M-RULES; path to rules/ must resolve from project root.
packet-example.field-8: V-MP-RULES verification excerpt verbatim: 12 scenarios (1-12), 2 trace markers (BLOCK_LOAD_RULES, BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE), 3 forbidden behaviors, 5 module checks.
packet-example.field-9: mp_clean_env (autouse), caplog_at_info, marker_counter, tmp_path. DO NOT redefine.
packet-example.field-11: 2 trace markers: [MP-Rules][all_rules][BLOCK_LOAD_RULES] doc_format=%s rule_count=%d at INFO; [MP-Rules][evaluate][BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE] rule_id=%s check=%s at INFO. Wrap in # START_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES / # END_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES; # START_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE / # END_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
packet-example.field-12: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_rules.py -x; ruff check src/mint_python/rules/ tests/unit/test_mp_rules.py; mypy --strict src/mint_python/rules/; python -c "from mint_python.rules import all_rules; assert len(all_rules(doc_format='docx')) > 0"; python -c "import mint_python.rules; assert all(n in dir(mint_python.rules) for n in ['Rule','Violation','Severity','FixCategory','evaluate','all_rules'])"
packet-example.field-14: knowledge-graph.xml MP-RULES STATUS planned→done (controller flips post-wave).
packet-example.field-15: V-MP-RULES scenarios 1-12 status planned→done; check-1..5 status planned→done.
packet-example.field-16: `grace(MP-RULES): land Rule/Violation types + YAML loader + XPath evaluate for Phase-9`

packet-example.field-4: src/mint_python/validate.py (new file). New test file: tests/unit/test_mp_validate.py. NO edits to any other src/mint_python/* file, NO edits to docs/*.xml, NO edits to conftest.py or _mp_helpers.py.
packet-example.field-5: MP-VALIDATE: Run all OOXML rules against a saved .docx with severity mode enforcement. ValidationReport dataclass. SeverityMode enum (AUDIT/LENIENT/STRICT) — self-defined, decoupled from mint.config. run_checks(document_path, severity_mode, rules_dir) → ValidationReport. validate(document_path, severity_mode, rules_dir) → ValidationReport (convenience).
packet-example.field-7: MP-VALIDATE depends on MP-RULES (Rule, Violation, Severity, FixCategory, evaluate, all_rules, get_hint). MP-RULES was shipped in Wave-9-1 — all types and functions are importable. Uses lxml for XML parsing from .docx zip; uses zipfile for ZIP extraction; uses pyyaml transitively via MP-RULES.
packet-example.field-8: V-MP-VALIDATE verification excerpt: 10 scenarios, 1 trace marker (BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS), 3 forbidden behaviors, 5 module checks.

packet-example.field-5: MP-FIX: Apply safe/visual auto-fixes to OOXML documents with backup, cascade detection, destructive rejection. FixReport dataclass. FixError, CascadeDetectedError, BackupFailedError, DestructiveRejectedError. DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS=3. create_backup(path). apply_fixes(path, violations, max_iterations, rules_dir) → FixReport. fix(path, rules_dir) → FixReport.
packet-example.field-7: MP-FIX depends on MP-VALIDATE (run_checks, ValidationReport, SeverityMode, InvalidDocumentError) and MP-RULES (FixCategory, Violation, Severity). Both were shipped in Wave-9-1 and Wave-9-2. Uses zipfile + io for in-place docx modification; uses shutil for backup copy.
packet-example.field-8: V-MP-FIX verification excerpt: 7 scenarios, 2 trace markers (BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP, BLOCK_APPLY_FIX), 4 forbidden behaviors, 4 module checks.
packet-example.field-9: mp_clean_env (autouse), caplog_at_info, marker_counter, tmp_path. Read-only access to tests/fixtures/raw_newline.docx. DO NOT redefine.
packet-example.field-11: 2 trace markers: [MP-Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] backup_path=... at INFO; [MP-Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] iterations=%d applied=%s diff=%s at INFO. Wrap in # START_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP / # END_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP; # START_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX / # END_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX.
packet-example.field-12: pytest tests/unit/test_mp_fix.py -x; ruff check src/mint_python/fix.py tests/unit/test_mp_fix.py; mypy --strict src/mint_python/fix.py; python -c "from mint_python.fix import fix, DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS; assert DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS == 3"

NO edits to any other src/mint_python/core/* file. NO edits to conftest.py (existing Phase-7+Phase-8 fixtures sufficient).
packet-example.field-5: Unstub Document.validate(level) and Document.fix(strategy). Delegation pattern: validate saves to temp .docx → calls MP-VALIDATE.validate(tmp_path, level) → returns ValidationReport; fix saves to temp → calls MP-VALIDATE.run_checks → MP-FIX.apply_fixes → returns FixReport. Temp files cleaned up via contextlib after call.
packet-example.field-7: Document methods depend on MP-VALIDATE (validate, run_checks, ValidationReport, SeverityMode) and MP-FIX (fix, apply_fixes, FixReport). Both shipped in Wave-9-2 and Wave-9-3. Additionally depends on MP-SECTION (Section) and MP-STYLE — already shipped Phase-7/8.

packet-example.field-9: mp_clean_env, tmp_docx_path, caplog_at_info, marker_counter, golden_doc_builder. DO NOT redefine.
packet-example.field-11: NO new trace markers. Existing BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX fires from Document.save() (unchanged). BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD fires ONLY for inject_grace and to_pdf — specifically NOT for validate/fix.

packet-example.expected-evidence: `assert_matplotlib_backend_is_agg()` after `import mint_python.core.chart` returns silently (backend == 'agg')
packet-example.observed-evidence: AssertionError: backend is 'TkAgg' — chart.py module-level forced 'Agg' but a sibling test file imported matplotlib.pyplot earlier in the test order, and matplotlib's import-once-set-once semantics held the prior backend
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/core/chart.py top — `import matplotlib; matplotlib.use('Agg')` MUST occur BEFORE `import matplotlib.pyplot as plt`. If chart.py uses `from matplotlib import pyplot as plt` first, the backend is locked before .use('Agg') has a chance.
packet-example.next-action: Reorder chart.py imports: line 1 `import matplotlib`, line 2 `matplotlib.use('Agg')`, line 3 `import matplotlib.pyplot as plt`. If a sibling test imports matplotlib.pyplot before chart.py loads, fix the ordering in that test (push the import inside the test function OR add `matplotlib.use('Agg')` to a session-scope conftest fixture). Verify by running tests in deterministic order: `pytest -p no:randomly tests/unit/`.

packet-example.scenario: V-MP-CHART forbidden-3: seaborn imported at module level
packet-example.expected-evidence: `'seaborn' not in sys.modules` after `import mint_python.core.chart` (lazy-import contract)
packet-example.observed-evidence: seaborn IS in sys.modules — V-MP-CHART check-4 fails ("python -c 'import sys; import mint_python.core.chart; assert seaborn not in sys.modules'")
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/core/chart.py top — somebody added `import seaborn` at module level OR a transitive dependency (one of the factory helpers) imports seaborn. Greppable: `grep -n 'import seaborn' src/mint_python/core/chart.py` returns the offending line.
packet-example.next-action: Remove the module-level `import seaborn`. Move the import inside Chart.from_seaborn body (after the `try:` block that catches ImportError → re-raises with the "pip install seaborn" hint). Re-run check-4. seaborn must remain optional per technology.xml.

packet-example.scenario: V-MP-CHART forbidden-5: rcParams leak across tests
packet-example.expected-evidence: mpl_rcparams_snapshot fixture asserts `dict(matplotlib.rcParams) == snapshot` at teardown
packet-example.observed-evidence: AssertionError at teardown: rcParams['font.family'] != snapshot value — Chart factory modified rcParams without restoring
packet-example.first-divergent-block: chart.py factory body — corporate theming applies rcParams DIRECTLY (`matplotlib.rcParams['font.family'] = ...`) instead of inside a `with mpl.rc_context(...):` block. The modification leaks to the next test.
packet-example.next-action: Wrap theming application in `with mpl.rc_context({...}):` (matplotlib's stdlib context manager). All factory savefig calls must happen INSIDE the context block. Verify with: write a test that builds 3 charts in sequence and asserts rcParams identical before/after each.

Wave-8-2 worker scope said "in-place add_chart unstub ONLY — preserve all other methods"; the worker likely refactored Section.render or _blocks initialization as a side effect.

The Wave-8-2 surgical edit should ONLY touch the body of add_chart (3-5 lines: drop the BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD logger.info + raise; replace with `self._blocks.append(chart); return self`). Re-run both VF-013 e2e and VF-014 e2e — both must pass.

packet-example.expected-evidence: doc.inline_shapes[i].width.emu == round(width_inches[i] × 914400) for each chart

packet-example.first-divergent-block: chart.py Chart.render — width converted to EMU via float arithmetic that loses precision: `int(width_inches * 914400)` instead of `round(width_inches * 914400)`. python-docx's Inches() helper does the right thing; bypassing it loses precision.
packet-example.next-action: Pass width via `docx.shared.Inches(self.width_inches)` directly to add_picture (NOT a hand-computed EMU value). Inches converts via 914400 multiplication then int-rounds. Re-run VF-014 inv-3.

Re-run V-MP-AUTH-SHIM scenario-2 + VF-017 inv-1 byte-equality assertion. Mirrors V-MP-FIX forbidden-2 fix-pattern: destructive check at the top, before backup.

Add a separate clean_writers_config fixture that clears the cache between tests (reload module via importlib.reload OR expose a _reset_for_tests() hook). Re-run VF-017 inv-2 with N=5.
