Add a regex assertion to V-MP-AUTH-SHIM scenario-5 verifying the rendered message is free of allowlist entries (use a fixture that injects a sentinel writer name and asserts the sentinel never appears in caplog).

packet-example.scenario: VF-018 inv-2: READ-ONLY broken on mint_read_grace_manifest
packet-example.expected-evidence: sha256(open(path, 'rb').read()) before mint_read_grace_manifest(path) call equals sha256(open(path, 'rb').read()) after the call returns
packet-example.observed-evidence: Hashes differ — the read path opened the docx with zipfile.ZipFile(path, 'a') instead of mode='r', and zipfile rewrote the central directory
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/mcp/manifest.py (or MP-GRACE.describe upstream) — ZipFile constructor missing explicit mode='r'; zipfile defaults to 'r' but if a ZipFile instance is later passed to a writer helper, the file is reopened in append mode
packet-example.next-action: Audit every zipfile.ZipFile call on the read path; pin mode='r' explicitly. Add a regression test that wraps the ZipFile class with a sentinel asserting __init__ kwargs.get('mode', 'r') in {'r'}. Re-run VF-018 inv-2 byte-equality assertion across all 6 V-MP-MANIFEST-READ scenarios.

packet-example.scenario: VF-018 inv-5: MOST-RECENT-WINS broken with multiple manifest parts
packet-example.expected-evidence: Docx with grace/manifest_v1.xml (timestamp T1) + grace/manifest_v2.xml (timestamp T2 > T1) → mint_read_grace_manifest returns the v2 dict (audit_id from T2)
packet-example.observed-evidence: Returned dict carries the v1 audit_id — implementation iterates with a dict-from-zip ordering that doesn't sort by timestamp
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/mcp/manifest.py — manifest selection uses sorted(zipfile.namelist()) as a proxy for chronology. Filename suffix doesn't necessarily correlate with timestamp (Phase-14 W3 names them with the audit_id, not a counter); the right key is the parsed manifest's timestamp field
packet-example.next-action: Replace the namelist-based selection with: parse all grace/manifest_*.xml entries → sort by parsed manifest.timestamp DESC → return [0]. Add a test fixture that produces a docx with two manifests where the namelist order is INVERTED relative to timestamp (e.g. v1 written second but with an older synthetic timestamp). Re-run VF-018 inv-5.

packet-example.scenario: VF-019 inv-1: ADVISORY-ONLY broken — hook raises into create_document
packet-example.expected-evidence: create_document(intent='draft memo', doc_type='memo') with backend unavailable returns success=True; structured_content.visual_qa.skipped is True; no exception propagates to the MCP client
packet-example.observed-evidence: create_document raised VISUAL_QA_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE — the hook used `raise` instead of returning None, breaking the advisory-only contract
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/qa/visual.py score_document() — the backend-probe failure path raises VisualQAError instead of logging+returning None. The hook caller in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py also lacks a try/except around score_document; even if score_document raises, the create_document path must catch and degrade.
packet-example.next-action: Two-layer defense: (1) score_document MUST never raise — replace `raise VisualQAError(...)` with `logger.warning(...); return None`. (2) The create_document hook caller wraps score_document in try/except Exception — log + degrade, never propagate. The advisory contract is explicit at BOTH layers because Phase-15 ships a 1-call-site hook today but Phase-16+ may add more callers. Re-run VF-019 inv-1 across all 6 V-MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK scenarios; pytest.raises(VisualQAError) MUST NOT fire on any of them.

packet-example.scenario: VF-019 inv-2: POSITION-AFTER-GRACE broken — QA runs before GRACE injection
packet-example.expected-evidence: assert_marker_sequence(caplog, ["BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST", "BLOCK_QA_SCORE"]) holds on every successful create_document call
packet-example.observed-evidence: BLOCK_QA_SCORE fires BEFORE BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST — Wave-15-2 worker integrated the hook before Document.inject_grace, so the visually-scored file lacks its GRACE manifest
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/mcp/document.py create_document() pipeline — the score_document call site is between save() and inject_grace() instead of after both
packet-example.next-action: Move the score_document hook AFTER MP-GRACE.bootstrap completes. The visually-scored file MUST carry its GRACE manifest because: (a) the manifest is part of the document's "shipped" identity; (b) future audits may visually compare against the manifest's preset/template_version metadata. Re-run VF-019 inv-2 + forbidden-1 + forbidden-5.

packet-example.expected-evidence: VLM HTTP request body (captured via fake_vlm fixture's request introspector) contains the rendered PNG (base64) and the fixed rubric prompt; NO sentinel string from the caller's intent appears anywhere in the captured body
packet-example.observed-evidence: The captured request body contains a substring of the caller's intent (e.g. the sentinel "MINT_TEST_INTENT_SENTINEL_42") — a "context-aware" prompt formatting interpolated the create_document intent into the rubric for "better scoring"
packet-example.first-divergent-block: src/mint_python/qa/visual.py — the rubric prompt builder takes the intent or fields_elicited as a parameter for "context", interpolating it into the VLM prompt. This violates the security boundary: visual-QA evaluates the RENDERED document, not the user's intent. The caller's data must not leak to an external VLM endpoint.
packet-example.next-action: Strip ALL caller-derived parameters from the VLM prompt builder. The rubric is a CONSTANT — same prompt for every call, parameterized only by page_no / total_pages / preset_name (not the user's intent). Re-run VF-019 inv-7 with multiple sentinel injections; the captured body must remain sentinel-free across all variants. Document the constraint in src/mint_python/qa/visual.py module contract: "The VLM prompt is fixed and contains NO caller-supplied data."

packet-example.scenario: V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION scenario-5: dangling import in tools/article_experiment
packet-example.expected-evidence: grep -rn "from src\.mint\.mcp_g[12]\|import src\.mint\.mcp_g[12]\|from src\.mint\.sandbox\|import src\.mint\.sandbox" src/ tests/ tools/ scripts/ returns zero hits
packet-example.observed-evidence: tools/article_experiment/run_experiment.py:14: from src.mint.mcp_g2 import mint_create — Wave-15-3 worker deleted the legacy modules without auditing tools/ first
packet-example.first-divergent-block: controller-pre-flight on Wave-15-3 was skipped — the grep audit was supposed to run BEFORE deletion proceeds (verification-plan.xml SwarmExecutionReadiness/target-15/controller-pre-flight)
packet-example.next-action: Restore the deleted modules in a single rollback commit. Run the grep BEFORE re-attempting deletion. Migrate the article_experiment runner to mint_python.mcp.document.create_document (or delete the runner if it's no longer used per memory project_article_experiment status). Document any external consumer in the PR description so the deletion is traceable. Forbidden-3 in V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION exists exactly to catch this — the worker who skipped pre-flight violated the protocol.

packet-example.scenario: V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION forbidden-1: W3 dispatched before W1+W2 merged
packet-example.expected-evidence: Wave-15-3 worker dispatch occurs ONLY after Wave-15-1 (MP-AUTH-SHIM + MP-MANIFEST-READ) AND Wave-15-2 (MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK) have merged green to main
packet-example.observed-evidence: Controller dispatched Wave-15-3 in parallel with Wave-15-2 to "save time"; the W3 worker's deletion of src/mint/cli.py touched lines that Wave-15-2 needed to add the visual-QA-hook feature flag
packet-example.first-divergent-block: SwarmExecutionReadiness/target-15/wave-dependencies — wave ordering Wave-15-1 → Wave-15-2 → Wave-15-3 is contractually serial; controller violated the ordering
packet-example.next-action: Stop Wave-15-3, revert the deletion commits. Land Wave-15-2 fully on main first. Then redispatch Wave-15-3 against the post-W2 main. Document the dispatch order as a controller-side check before any future Phase-15-style deprecation phase.
